description: Creation of the heavens, earth, angels, jinn, Adam ﷺ and the early world

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: From what substance were the Jinn created, according to the Quran and authentic hadith?
questions.explanation: The Quran describes jinn as being created from "a smokeless flame of fire" (Surah Ar-Rahman 55:15), distinguishing their nature from humans, who were created from clay. This highlights their spiritual and unseen characteristics, as fire is intangible and dynamic.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: How old was Adam when he died, according to the narrations cited by Ibn Kathir?
opts: His age is not mentioned in authentic narrations
questions.explanation: According to Ibn Kathir's narrations, Prophet Adam ﷺ lived for 1000 years before he passed away. This duration is mentioned in various Islamic traditions to emphasize the long lifespan of the first human created by Allah.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What was the immediate consequence of eating from the tree?
opts: Their private parts became apparent to them, and they began to cover themselves with Paradise leaves
They were immediately removed from Paradise
questions.explanation: After eating from the forbidden tree, Adam and Hawwa (Eve) became aware of their nakedness, symbolizing the loss of their initial innocence, so they covered themselves with leaves from Paradise to conceal their private parts. This marked the beginning of human self-awareness and modesty.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: Which angel is entrusted with the blowing of the Trumpet (Sur) on the Day of Judgment?
questions.explanation: Israfil (Raphael) is the angel assigned to blow the Trumpet (Sur) on the Day of Judgment, signaling the end of the world and the resurrection of all beings. This role is unique and significant, marking the transition from worldly life to the hereafter as described in Islamic teachings.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: What was the occupation of Habil (Abel) and Qabil (Cain)?
opts: Habil was a farmer, Qabil was a shepherd
Habil was a shepherd, Qabil was a farmer
questions.explanation: Habil (Abel) was a shepherd who tended animals, while Qabil (Cain) was a farmer who cultivated the land. This distinction is mentioned in Islamic tradition to highlight their different livelihoods and the context of their offerings to Allah.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: According to Surah al-Isra (17:64-65), what resources did Allah permit Iblis to use?
opts: His voice, his cavalry and infantry, sharing in wealth and children, and making promises
questions.explanation: Allah allowed Iblis to use his voice to whisper and deceive, as well as his cavalry and infantry, symbolizing his ability to influence people through various means like wealth, children, and false promises, to lead them astray as mentioned in Surah al-Isra (17:64-65).

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: How was the acceptance of the sacrifice shown?
opts: Fire came down from heaven and consumed Habil's sacrifice but left Qabil's
questions.explanation: The acceptance of the sacrifice was shown by fire descending from heaven to consume Habil's offering, indicating Allah's approval, while Qabil's sacrifice was left untouched, signifying rejection. This event highlights divine acceptance based on sincerity and righteousness.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What did Allah create to adorn the lowest heaven, as mentioned in Surah al-Mulk and Surah al-Saffat?
opts: Planets and stars as missiles against devils
questions.explanation: Allah created planets and stars to adorn the lowest heaven and to serve as missiles that drive away devils, as mentioned in Surah al-Mulk (67:5) and Surah al-Saffat (37:6-10). This highlights both the beauty and the protective function of celestial bodies in Islamic cosmology.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What does the study reveal about Ibn Kathir's criticism of hadith found in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim?
opts: He never criticized any hadith from them
He subjected nineteen Sahihayn hadiths to content criticism, revealing incorrectness in some and explaining problematic points in others
He considered all hadith in them immune to criticism
He only praised them without any analysis
questions.explanation: Ibn Kathir critically examined nineteen hadiths from Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim, identifying inaccuracies or clarifying difficult aspects. This shows that even highly regarded collections are subject to scholarly analysis to ensure precise understanding and authenticity.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: How long did Adam live on earth, according to the most famous narrations?
opts: His age is not mentioned in authentic narrations
questions.explanation: According to well-known Islamic narrations, Prophet Adam lived on earth for about 1000 years, allowing him to teach his descendants and establish the early human community. This duration highlights his significant role as the first human and prophet in Islamic tradition.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What was Adam's final advice to his children before his death?
opts: To worship Allah alone and follow His messengers
questions.explanation: Adam's final advice emphasized monotheism and obedience, urging his children to worship Allah alone and follow His messengers, ensuring they remain on the straight path and uphold the teachings revealed to humanity. This guidance highlights the core principle of Islam: sincere devotion to Allah and adherence to His guidance.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: In the hadith about the Prophet's ﷺ visit to Ta'if, which angel was sent to him by Allah, and what offer did he make?
opts: Jibril, offering to bring the mountains of Ta'if to him
Mika'il, offering to send rain upon the people
The Angel of the Mountains, offering to crush the people between the two mountains
Israfil, offering to destroy the city with a blast
questions.explanation: During the Prophet's ﷺ visit to Ta'if, the Angel of the Mountains was sent by Allah and offered to crush the people between the two mountains as punishment for their rejection. However, the Prophet ﷺ prayed for their guidance instead, showing his mercy and patience.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Pen & The Tablet
questions.q: What was the very first thing Allah created, according to authentic narrations mentioned in Volume 1?
questions.explanation: The very first thing Allah created was the Pen (Al-Qalam), which He commanded to write everything that will happen until the Day of Judgment. This emphasizes Allah’s knowledge and control over all events, as recorded in authentic narrations from early Islamic sources like Al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What are the names of the angels who record the deeds of every person?
questions.explanation: The angels who record every person's deeds are called Kiraman Katibin, consisting of two angels: one records good deeds (Raqib) and the other records bad deeds (Atid). They document all actions to be presented on the Day of Judgment.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: What was Iblis's explicit reason for refusing to prostrate to Adam?
opts: He claimed he was created from fire while Adam was from clay, and fire is superior
He thought prostration should be for Allah alone (pretending piety)
He was jealous that Adam was created after him
questions.explanation: Iblis refused to prostrate to Adam because he believed his creation from fire was superior to Adam's creation from clay, showing arrogance and pride. This reasoning led to his disobedience and expulsion from God's mercy.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: What is the Covenant of Alast mentioned in Surah al-A'raf (7:172)?
opts: The covenant Allah made with the prophets
The covenant Allah took from the children of Adam, making them testify that He is their Lord
The covenant between Adam and Hawwa
The covenant between Muslims and Allah
questions.explanation: The Covenant of Alast refers to the moment when Allah gathered all the descendants of Adam and made them testify to His Lordship, affirming their recognition of Him as their Creator and Sustainer before their earthly life began. This establishes human accountability to Allah from the very beginning.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: Where did Allah place Adam and his wife after creating them?
questions.explanation: After creating Adam and his wife, Allah placed them in Paradise (Jannah) as a beautiful and peaceful abode where they could live and enjoy His blessings before being sent to Earth. This setting symbolizes the initial perfect state of human existence.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: What was the name of the person who first suggested making images of the righteous?
opts: A man named Yarub or something similar, according to some narrations
questions.explanation: A man named Yarub is mentioned in some narrations as the first to suggest making images of the righteous, aiming to preserve their memory. This reflects early human efforts to honor and remember virtuous individuals through visual representation.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What did Allah create to adorn the lowest heaven, as mentioned in several Quranic verses quoted by Ibn Kathir?
opts: Stars and planets as missiles against devils
questions.explanation: Allah created stars and planets to adorn the lowest heaven and to serve as missiles against devils who try to eavesdrop on the heavenly assembly, as explained by Ibn Kathir based on Quranic verses like Surah Al-Jinn (72:8-9). This highlights both their beauty and protective function.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: According to the hadith of Abu Hurairah, on which day did Allah create the mountains?
questions.explanation: Allah created the mountains on Sunday, as mentioned in the hadith narrated by Abu Hurairah. This sequence highlights the orderly process of creation, where Allah formed the earth first, then the mountains, demonstrating His wisdom in preparing the world for life.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: According to the hadith of Abu Dharr, what is the sequence from the earth where humans live up to the Throne?
opts: Earth → second earth → ... seventh earth → first heaven → ... seventh heaven → Kursi → Throne
Earth → first heaven → second heaven → ... seventh heaven → Kursi → Throne
questions.explanation: The hadith of Abu Dharr describes a hierarchical order starting from the earth where humans live, ascending through the seven heavens, then reaching the Kursi, and finally the Throne, illustrating the vastness and layered structure of the universe in Islamic cosmology.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: On which day of creation were the mountains created, according to the hadith of Abu Hurairah?
questions.explanation: According to the hadith of Abu Hurairah, mountains were created on the second day of creation, not Sunday. This highlights the sequence of Allah's creation, where the earth and its features, including mountains, were formed early to stabilize the land.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: When did Allah take this covenant from humanity?
opts: When Allah created Adam, He extracted all his descendants from his back and made them testify
During the lifetime of each prophet
questions.explanation: Allah took the covenant from all human souls when He created Adam by bringing forth their descendants from his back and making them testify to His Lordship, ensuring their acknowledgment of His oneness before their earthly existence.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: Which of the following is NOT covered in Volume 1 of Al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya?
opts: The creation of the Throne and Kursi
The story of Habil and Qabil
The creation of the Jinn and Iblis
questions.explanation: Volume 1 of Al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya primarily covers the beginning of creation, the stories of early prophets, and foundational events, but the detailed story of Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) is found in later volumes, as his narrative occurs after earlier prophetic histories are established.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: How did Iblis enter Paradise to deceive Adam and Hawwa after being expelled?
opts: He hid in the mouth of the snake
questions.explanation: Iblis entered Paradise by hiding in the mouth of the snake, allowing him to approach Adam and Hawwa unnoticed and deceive them. This method highlights his cunning and the subtle ways he sought to lead them astray after being expelled.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What was the test that led to Iblis's fall?
opts: He was asked to fast for a thousand years
He was commanded to prostrate to Adam
He was asked to surrender his position
questions.explanation: Iblis was commanded by Allah to prostrate to Adam as a sign of respect and obedience, but he refused out of arrogance, claiming he was superior because he was created from fire while Adam was created from clay. This disobedience led to his fall from grace.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: Which Surah in the Quran describes a group of Jinn listening to the Quran and believing?
questions.explanation: Surah al-Jinn (Chapter 72) describes a group of jinn who listen to the Quran being recited and accept its message, demonstrating their belief. This Surah highlights that jinn, like humans, have free will and can choose to believe or disbelieve in Allah’s guidance.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What does the hadith say about the angel's inclination?
opts: To promise good and affirm the truth
questions.explanation: Angels are described in hadith as beings who naturally incline towards promising good and affirming the truth, reflecting their pure and obedient nature in carrying out Allah's commands without error or falsehood.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: How many angels are assigned to every person to record deeds?
opts: Two angels - one on the right and one on the left
Four angels - two during the day and two at night
b and c combined - two recording angels plus the guardian angels
questions.explanation: Every person is assigned two angels, known as Kiraman Katibin, who record good and bad deeds, along with guardian angels who protect and watch over the individual. This combination ensures both the documentation of actions and personal protection throughout life.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: In what forms did Jibril sometimes appear to the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ?
opts: Sometimes as a handsome companion (Dihya al-Kalbi) and sometimes as a Bedouin
questions.explanation: Jibril sometimes appeared to the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in the form of Dihya al-Kalbi, a handsome companion known to the Prophet, and other times as a simple Bedouin, to convey messages discreetly and make the encounters more relatable. This shows the angel’s ability to assume different human forms.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Pen & The Tablet
questions.q: According to the authentic narrations compiled by Ibn Kathir, what was the very first thing Allah created?
questions.explanation: The very first thing Allah created was the Pen (Qalam), as it was commanded to write everything that would happen until the Day of Judgment. This emphasizes Allah’s knowledge and control over all events from the beginning of time.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: In the hadith about the "Lammah" (inclination) of the angel and the devil, what is the angel's inclination?
opts: To promise good and affirm the truth
questions.explanation: The angel's inclination is to encourage good deeds and confirm the truth, guiding humans towards righteousness. This contrasts with the devil's inclination, which is to incite evil and falsehood, highlighting the spiritual struggle between good and evil influences.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: In which seven locations in the Quran does Allah mention the story of Iblis's refusal to prostrate to Adam?
opts: Al-Baqarah, Al-A'raf, Al-Hijr, Al-Isra', Al-Kahf, Ta-Ha, and Saad
In the Torah and Quran only
questions.explanation: The story of Iblis's refusal to prostrate to Adam is mentioned in seven Quranic chapters to emphasize the significance of obedience and humility. These chapters—Al-Baqarah, Al-A'raf, Al-Hijr, Al-Isra', Al-Kahf, Ta-Ha, and Saad—highlight different aspects of the event and its lessons for mankind.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: According to the hadith narrated by Abu Hurairah about the creation days, on which day did Allah create the heavens?
opts: They were created in two days, but the hadith specifies them on Tuesday and Wednesday
questions.explanation: The hadith narrated by Abu Hurairah indicates that Allah created the heavens over two days, specifically on Tuesday and Wednesday, highlighting the detailed stages of creation within the six-day framework mentioned in Islamic teachings. This shows the methodical and purposeful nature of Allah’s creation process.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What fell from Jibril's wings when the Prophet ﷺ saw him in his original form?
questions.explanation: When the Prophet ﷺ saw Jibril in his original, majestic form, the immense light and power caused pearls and rubies to fall from his wings, symbolizing the angel's divine beauty and the awe-inspiring nature of his true appearance.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: How did Allah respond to the angels' question about creating Adam?
opts: He explained that Adam would have prophets among his descendants
He said "I know that which you do not know"
He became angry and silenced them
He created Adam immediately to prove them wrong
questions.explanation: When the angels questioned Allah about creating Adam, He responded, "I know that which you do not know," indicating His infinite wisdom and knowledge beyond their understanding, especially regarding Adam's unique role and potential in creation.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: How did Iblis deceive Adam and Hawwa into eating from the forbidden tree?
opts: He whispered to them, calling it the "Tree of Immortality" and promising that they would become angels or live forever
He made it look beautiful and irresistible
He told them Allah had changed His command
questions.explanation: Iblis deceived Adam and Hawwa by whispering false promises that eating from the forbidden tree would grant them immortality or angelic status, appealing to their desire for eternal life and higher rank, which led them to disobey Allah's command.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What did all the descendants of Adam testify?
opts: "We bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah"
"Am I not your Lord?" They said, "Yes, we bear witness"
"Paradise is true and Hell is true"
questions.explanation: All the descendants of Adam testified to the oneness of Allah by acknowledging His Lordship with the words, "Am I not your Lord?" and their response, "Yes, we bear witness," as mentioned in the Quran (7:172), affirming their innate recognition of Allah before entering the world.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What was Iblis's rank among the angels?
opts: He was the leader of the angels
He was a teacher of the angels
He was a renowned worshipper and scholar among them
He was the lowest among them
questions.explanation: Iblis was considered a distinguished worshipper and knowledgeable scholar among the angels due to his devoutness and understanding, which made his refusal to bow to Adam more significant as it was an act of pride despite his esteemed status.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What are the angels called who protect a person from harm by Allah's command?
questions.explanation: Al-Mu'aqqibat are angels assigned by Allah to protect and guard individuals from harm continuously, succeeding one another in this duty. They ensure a person's safety by Allah's command, as mentioned in the Quran (Surah Ar-Ra’d, 13:11).

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: According to Surah al-Ra'd (13:11), what do the Mu'aqqibat do?
opts: They protect a person from behind and before, by the command of Allah
questions.explanation: The Mu'aqqibat are angels assigned to guard and protect a person from all directions, both in front and behind, ensuring safety by Allah’s command. This highlights Allah’s constant care and control over human affairs through His appointed angels.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: What does "sharing in wealth and children" mean according to the commentators?
opts: Iblis owns half of all human wealth
Every wealth that is not purified by Zakah, and every child born out of wedlock or not named properly
questions.explanation: "Sharing in wealth and children" refers to possessing wealth that hasn't been purified through Zakah, which is obligatory charity, and having children born outside of lawful marriage or without proper naming, both of which affect the legitimacy and spiritual purity in Islamic teachings.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: Which of the following characteristics do the Jinn share with humans?
opts: They are made of the same substance
They eat, drink, marry, and have children
questions.explanation: Jinn share several characteristics with humans, such as eating, drinking, marrying, and having children, which shows they have similar social and physical needs. Unlike angels, jinn possess free will and live in communities, making them accountable for their actions like humans.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: Where did Iblis descend to earth?
questions.explanation: Iblis descended to earth at Dashtimaysan, near Basra, as mentioned in Islamic traditions and historical texts like Al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya. This location is significant because it marks the beginning of his influence on humanity after his expulsion from the heavens.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What is the relationship between the Throne (`Arsh) and the Kursi (Footstool)?
opts: The Kursi is inside the Throne and smaller
The Throne is above the Kursi and encompasses it, just as the Kursi encompasses the heavens
The Kursi is above the Throne
questions.explanation: The Throne (`Arsh) is positioned above the Kursi and encompasses it, symbolizing a greater magnitude, while the Kursi itself encompasses the heavens, indicating its vastness but lesser status compared to the Throne. This hierarchy reflects Allah’s supreme authority and the grandeur of His creation.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What is the name of the boundary tree at the end of the seventh heaven?
opts: The Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary (Sidrat al-Muntaha)
questions.explanation: The Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary (Sidrat al-Muntaha) marks the limit beyond which no creation can pass, located at the end of the seventh heaven. It symbolizes the boundary of divine knowledge and the closest point to Allah’s presence accessible to the Prophet Muhammad during the Isra and Mi'raj.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What is the name of the chief angel responsible for Hellfire (Jahannam)?
questions.explanation: Malik is the chief angel appointed as the guardian of Hellfire (Jahannam), responsible for overseeing its punishment and ensuring that the inhabitants remain there as decreed by Allah. This role is mentioned in the Quran, highlighting Malik's unique position among the angels.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: If Iblis was a jinn, how did he come to be among the angels in heaven?
opts: He worshipped with the angels due to his devotion and was elevated to their company
He captured an angel and took his place
He was an angel who turned into a jinn
questions.explanation: Iblis was a jinn, but because of his sincere worship and devotion, he was granted a place among the angels in heaven. His elevated status was due to his piety, not his nature, which later led to his refusal to bow to Adam.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: How many angels are the keepers (guards) of Hellfire, as mentioned in Surah al-Muddaththir?
questions.explanation: Surah al-Muddaththir (74:30) mentions that nineteen angels are appointed as keepers of Hellfire, emphasizing their role in guarding and managing the punishment for the disbelievers. This specific number highlights the severity and orderliness of Hell’s guardianship.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What statement of Hasan al-Basri does Ibn Kathir quote about Iblis and the angels?
opts: "Iblis was the leader of the angels in the first heaven"
"Iblis never had the power to even blink his eyes among the angels"
"Iblis was created from the same light as the angels but chose fire"
questions.explanation: Hasan al-Basri's statement, as quoted by Ibn Kathir, emphasizes that Iblis was never truly among the angels in status or power; he was always distinct and inferior, highlighting that Iblis was a jinn and not an angel, despite being in their company.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: How did the Prophet ﷺ respond to the Angel of the Mountains' offer to destroy the people of Ta'if?
opts: He accepted the offer to teach them a lesson
He asked the angel to wait until morning
He said he hoped that from their offspring would come people who worship Allah alone
He forgave them immediately and asked the angel to leave
questions.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ declined the Angel of the Mountains' offer to destroy Ta'if because he hoped that future generations from their descendants would embrace monotheism and worship Allah alone, showing his mercy and optimism for their guidance.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: How many children did Adam and Hawwa have, according to various narrations?
opts: The exact number is not confirmed in authentic sources, but they had many children
questions.explanation: According to various narrations, Adam and Hawwa had 40 children, consisting of 20 pairs of twins. This detail highlights the early human population growth as described in Islamic tradition.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: When was Adam created, according to this hadith?
opts: After the afternoon (`Asr) of Friday, as the last of creation
questions.explanation: Adam was created after the afternoon (`Asr) of Friday, making him the last of all creation. This timing highlights the special status of Friday and Adam’s unique role as the first human and final creation in the sequence.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: According to Ibn Kathir's methodology, what is his position on narrations that discuss what was created first when there are multiple authentic narrations?
opts: He accepts only one as correct and rejects others
He acknowledges that such matters are from the unseen and we accept all authentic narrations without attempting to reconcile impossible contradictions
He prefers Isra'iliyyat over Islamic narrations
questions.explanation: Ibn Kathir teaches that since the exact order of creation is part of the unseen, we accept all authentic narrations as they are without trying to resolve contradictions that cannot be logically harmonized, emphasizing faith in the revealed texts over speculative reasoning.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What is the name of the chief angel responsible for Paradise (Jannah)?
questions.explanation: Ridwan is the chief angel appointed to guard and oversee Paradise (Jannah), ensuring its sanctity and welcoming the righteous. This role distinguishes him from other angels with different responsibilities, such as Malik, who guards Hell.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: According to the same narration, how did Iblis end up in heaven with the angels?
opts: He was captured as a prisoner by the angels and taken with them to heaven
He ascended on his own power
questions.explanation: Iblis was taken to heaven because he was captured as a prisoner by the angels, who brought him along with them. This shows that initially, Iblis was among the angels in heaven due to this captivity before his rebellion.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: Who were Wadd, Suwa', Yaghuth, Ya'uq, and Nasr?
opts: Righteous people from the descendants of Adam who were later worshipped as idols
questions.explanation: Wadd, Suwa', Yaghuth, Ya'uq, and Nasr were originally righteous individuals from Adam's descendants, but over time, people began to worship them as idols instead of Allah, which is condemned in Islamic teachings. This highlights the danger of idol worship and deviation from monotheism.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: According to Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him), how long did Adam remain in Paradise before being sent down?
opts: Only between the afternoon prayer (`Asr) and sunset
questions.explanation: Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) explained that Prophet Adam ﷺ stayed in Paradise for a very short time, specifically between the `Asr (afternoon) prayer and sunset, highlighting the immediacy of his descent to Earth after creation. This emphasizes the swift transition from Paradise to earthly life.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: When Allah informed the angels that He was creating a vicegerent (khalifah) on earth, what was their response?
opts: They asked "Will You place therein one who will spread corruption and shed blood while we glorify You?"
They asked to be sent instead
questions.explanation: The angels questioned Allah about creating a vicegerent because they knew humans might cause corruption and bloodshed, contrasting with their own continuous glorification of Allah. This shows their concern about the potential negative impact of humans on earth.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What was the one thing Allah forbade Adam and Hawwa from doing in Paradise?
questions.explanation: Allah forbade Adam and Hawwa from eating the fruit of a specific tree in Paradise as a test of obedience. This command was meant to teach them submission to Allah’s will and the consequences of disobedience.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: What was the Prophet's ﷺ response to the Angel of the Mountains' offer to crush the people of Ta'if between the two mountains?
opts: He accepted the offer to teach them a lesson
He asked the angel to wait until morning
He said: "No, I hope that Allah will bring forth from their loins people who worship Allah alone, associating nothing with Him"
He forgave them immediately and asked the angel to leave
questions.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ declined the Angel's offer because he hoped for the guidance and repentance of the people of Ta'if, wishing that future generations from them would worship Allah alone without associating partners with Him, demonstrating his mercy and hope for their spiritual growth.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: What lies beneath the seventh earth, according to some narrations mentioned by Ibn Kathir?
opts: The mention of what lies beneath is from the unseen; we affirm what is mentioned in authentic texts and stop where they stop
questions.explanation: What lies beneath the seventh earth is part of the unseen knowledge that humans cannot fully comprehend; Islamic teachings emphasize affirming only what is clearly stated in authentic texts and refraining from speculation beyond them.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: From what substance were the angels created, according to the authentic hadith cited by Ibn Kathir?
questions.explanation: Angels were created from light (nur) according to authentic hadith reported by Ibn Kathir, distinguishing their nature from humans, who were created from clay, and jinn, who were created from smokeless fire. This highlights the spiritual and pure essence of angels in Islamic belief.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: Who was Shayth (Seth) and what was his significance?
opts: He was the righteous son given to Adam after Habil's death, and he became a prophet
He was an angel sent to help Adam
questions.explanation: Shayth (Seth) was the son Allah granted to Adam after the death of Habil (Abel), known for his righteousness. He is significant because he continued Adam's prophetic lineage, guiding early humanity in faith and worship.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: What did the Jinn say when they heard the Quran recited by the Prophet ﷺ?
opts: "We have heard a wondrous Quran that guides to righteousness, and we believe in it"
questions.explanation: When the jinn heard the Quran recited by the Prophet ﷺ, they recognized its divine guidance and truth, leading them to declare their belief and acknowledge its power to guide to righteousness, showing their acceptance of Islam.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: What is the function of the mountains in relation to the earth?
opts: To prevent the earth from shaking with its inhabitants
questions.explanation: Mountains act as stabilizers for the earth, preventing excessive shaking or movement. This helps maintain balance and stability for all living beings on the earth, as described in Islamic teachings and supported by natural observations.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: What did Iblis ask Allah after being cursed?
opts: To be given respite until the Day of Judgment
To be given another chance to prostrate
questions.explanation: Iblis asked Allah for respite until the Day of Judgment to have time to mislead humans and prove that they would not be grateful or obedient. This request highlights his role as a tempter and the ongoing test for humanity.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: Which angel is entrusted with delivering revelation to the prophets?
questions.explanation: Angel Jibril (Gabriel) is entrusted with delivering revelation from Allah to the prophets, serving as the divine messenger who conveys Allah’s guidance and commandments, as described in Islamic teachings and the Quran.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What was Allah's response to Iblis's refusal?
opts: "Then get out of it (Paradise/heaven), for indeed you are accursed"
questions.explanation: Allah commanded Iblis to leave Paradise because he disobeyed by refusing to bow to Adam, showing arrogance and defiance. This rejection led to Iblis being cursed and expelled as a consequence of his disobedience.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: Do the Jinn have prophets and messengers sent to them?
opts: No, prophets were only sent to humans
Yes, prophets were sent to the Jinn as well
Some prophets were sent to both humans and Jinn
The Jinn learn from human prophets only
questions.explanation: Prophets were sent to guide both humans and jinn, as mentioned in the Quran, where some jinn listened to the message of Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). This shows that divine guidance extends to both creations to lead them towards righteousness.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: What is Ibn Kathir's stated methodology regarding the inclusion of Isra'iliyyat (Israelite traditions) in his work?
opts: He includes all of them as authentic sources
He rejects all of them completely
He only includes what is permitted by Islamic law—what does not contradict the Quran and Sunnah, and what can neither be confirmed nor denied
He uses them as primary sources for prophetic stories
questions.explanation: Ibn Kathir includes Isra'iliyyat only when they do not contradict the Quran and Sunnah, ensuring his work remains authentic and reliable. He avoids unverified stories, using them cautiously when they cannot be confirmed or denied, maintaining adherence to Islamic principles.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: What was Habil's response when Qabil threatened to kill him?
opts: He said, "If you stretch your hand against me to kill me, I will not stretch my hand against you to kill you. Indeed, I fear Allah, Lord of the worlds"
questions.explanation: Habil responded with patience and fear of Allah, refusing to retaliate despite Qabil's threat. This shows his righteousness and trust in God's justice, highlighting the moral lesson of avoiding violence and maintaining faith even in difficult situations.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What covers the Sidrat al-Muntaha, as described in the hadith of Ibn Mas'ud?
questions.explanation: The Sidrat al-Muntaha is described as being covered by a vast number of colors, including green, yellow, and white, symbolizing its immense beauty and significance in the heavens, as mentioned in the hadith of Ibn Mas'ud. This illustrates the extraordinary nature of this celestial boundary.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What protection did Allah give to His sincere servants against Iblis?
opts: They are completely immune to his whispers
He has no authority over them
They cannot see Iblis, so he cannot approach them
questions.explanation: Allah granted sincere servants protection by limiting Iblis's power, meaning he has no authority over those who are truly devoted and obedient to Allah. This divine safeguard prevents Iblis from misleading or harming them directly.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: What was Iblis's original name before his disobedience?
opts: There is no authentic record of his original name
questions.explanation: Before his disobedience, Iblis was known as Azazil, a respected jinn who was elevated among the angels due to his piety. His name changed after he refused to bow to Adam, leading to his fall from grace and becoming known as Iblis.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What was entrusted to Shayth by Adam?
opts: Adam's knowledge, prophethood, and the instructions for future generations
questions.explanation: Shayth was entrusted by Adam with his knowledge, prophethood, and guidance to preserve and pass on to future generations, ensuring the continuation of divine teachings and wisdom after Adam's time. This responsibility established Shayth as a key figure in maintaining the spiritual legacy.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What did Iblis whisper to Adam and Hawwa?
opts: "Eat from this tree and you will become angels"
"Eat from this tree and you will live forever (khuld)"
"Eat from this tree and you will have eternal kingdom"
questions.explanation: Iblis whispered to Adam and Hawwa to eat from the forbidden tree by promising them immortality and a kingdom that never decays, tempting them to disobey Allah’s command. This highlights the nature of Iblis’s deception and the test of obedience given to them.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What is the relationship between the seven heavens and the seven earths?
opts: They are parallel with no connection
Each earth is inside each heaven
The heavens are above the earths, with each earth separated by distances similar to the heavens
questions.explanation: The seven heavens are positioned above the seven earths, and between each earth there is a distance comparable to that between the heavens. This illustrates the vast and structured nature of creation as described in Islamic cosmology.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: When Ibn Kathir includes weak hadith in his work, what is his purpose according to his own statement?
opts: To rely upon them for rulings
To simplify summarized material or clarify something, but not as evidence to rely upon—merely as adornment
To challenge other scholars to authenticate them
questions.explanation: Ibn Kathir includes weak hadith to make complex topics easier to understand or to illustrate points more clearly, but he does not use them as strong evidence. Their purpose is decorative, not authoritative, ensuring readers are aware they should not rely on these hadith for firm rulings.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: How did Qabil kill Habil?
opts: He struck him with a rock or a piece of iron while he was sleeping
He pushed him off a cliff
questions.explanation: Qabil killed Habil by striking him with a rock or a piece of iron while Habil was sleeping, marking the first murder in human history according to Islamic tradition. This act symbolizes the beginning of human conflict and the consequences of jealousy and wrongdoing.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: What do the names Wadd, Suwa', Yaghuth, Ya'uq, and Nasr mean according to some interpretations?
opts: They were the actual names of the righteous men
They are names of idols only
questions.explanation: Wadd, Suwa', Yaghuth, Ya'uq, and Nasr are often understood as the names of idols worshipped by certain tribes, but some interpretations suggest they were originally the names of righteous men whose followers later deified them. This reflects how human figures became objects of worship over time.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: According to the hadith where the Prophet ﷺ saw Jibril in his original form, how many wings did Jibril have, and what was their span?
opts: Two wings, covering the entire earth
Six hundred wings, each covering the horizon
One thousand wings, covered with pearls and rubies
Seventy thousand wings, each dripping with light
questions.explanation: Jibril (Gabriel) was described in a hadith as having six hundred wings, each so vast that they covered the horizon, illustrating his immense power and grandeur as an angel. This emphasizes the majesty and awe-inspiring nature of angels in Islamic teachings.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: According to the academic study of Ibn Kathir's content criticism in the Sira section, which criteria did he use to criticize narrations?
opts: Conflict with Quran, authentic hadith, reason, historical facts, and inclusion of Isra'iliyyat
Only agreement with the majority of scholars
questions.explanation: Ibn Kathir critically evaluated narrations by checking their consistency with the Quran, authentic hadith, logical reasoning, established historical facts, and whether they contained Isra'iliyyat (Jewish or Christian traditions), ensuring only reliable and accurate reports were accepted in his Sira.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: Where was Adam buried, according to the most famous narrations?
opts: In Makkah, in the Cave of Abu Qubays or near the Kaaba
questions.explanation: According to well-known narrations, Adam was buried in Makkah, specifically in the Cave of Abu Qubays or near the Kaaba, highlighting the spiritual significance of this location as the first place of human worship and connection to Allah.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: Which angel is entrusted with blowing the Trumpet (Sur) on the Day of Judgment?
questions.explanation: Israfil is the angel assigned to blow the Trumpet (Sur) to signal the Day of Judgment, marking the end of the world and the resurrection of all beings. This role is unique and significant, as it initiates the final events in Islamic eschatology.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: Which of the following is mentioned in Volume 1 as being created before the heavens and earth?
opts: The Pen, water, and the Throne
questions.explanation: The Pen, water, and the Throne are mentioned in Al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya as being created before the heavens and earth, highlighting their primordial existence in Islamic cosmology. This order emphasizes God's initial acts of creation before forming the physical universe.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: How did idol worship begin among the descendants of Adam?
opts: Some people made images of righteous people who had died, to remember them and be inspired, then later generations worshipped these images
questions.explanation: Idol worship began when some descendants of Adam created images of righteous deceased individuals to remember and seek inspiration from them. Over time, later generations started worshipping these images themselves, turning remembrance into idol worship.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: What logical fallacy did Iblis commit in his argument?
opts: He judged superiority by substance rather than by obedience to Allah's command
He forgot that Allah's command supersedes all reasoning
questions.explanation: Iblis committed multiple logical fallacies, including arrogance by refusing to obey Allah, false superiority by claiming he was better than Adam, and flawed reasoning by basing his refusal on his creation from fire versus Adam's from clay, which is not a valid basis for disobedience in Islamic teachings.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What does Surah al-Kahf (18:50) explicitly state about Iblis's origin?
opts: "He was from the jinn, so he disobeyed the command of his Lord"
"He was from the humans who were elevated"
questions.explanation: Surah al-Kahf (18:50) clarifies that Iblis was created from the jinn, not from angels, which explains why he had free will and chose to disobey Allah's command, unlike the angels who are inherently obedient.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: According to Surah Qaf (50:17-18), when do the two recording angels record deeds?
opts: One sits on the right and one on the left, and not a word does he utter except that with him is an observer ready [to record]
Only when the person is awake
questions.explanation: The verses in Surah Qaf (50:17-18) describe two angels assigned to each person, one on the right and one on the left, who continuously record every word and deed without missing anything, ensuring that all actions are documented for accountability on the Day of Judgment.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: What does the verse "Share with them in wealth and children" mean according to the commentators cited by Ibn Kathir?
opts: Iblis has the power to take people's money and children
Every wealth that is not purified by Zakah, and every child born out of wedlock or not named properly
Iblis owns half of all human wealth
Iblis can possess children at birth
questions.explanation: The verse "Share with them in wealth and children" refers to the idea that wealth not purified by Zakah and children born outside of lawful marriage or without proper naming do not truly belong to believers, highlighting the importance of lawful earnings and legitimate lineage in Islam.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What is the name of the chief angel responsible for Paradise?
questions.explanation: Ridwan is the chief angel appointed to guard and oversee Paradise, ensuring its sanctity and welcoming the righteous. This role distinguishes him from other angels who have different responsibilities in Islamic tradition.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What is the relationship between the seven heavens and the seven earths according to Ibn Kathir's compilation?
opts: They are parallel layers with no contact
The heavens are above the earths, with each earth separated by distances similar to the heavens
The earths are inside the first heaven
questions.explanation: According to Ibn Kathir, the seven heavens are positioned above the seven earths, and each earth is separated by distances comparable to those between the heavens. This illustrates the vastness and order of Allah's creation in the universe.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: What did Qabil do with Habil's body after killing him?
opts: He carried it for a long time, not knowing what to do with it
He left it where it fell
He threw it in a river
questions.explanation: After killing Habil, Qabil was confused and remorseful, so he carried his brother's body for a long time, uncertain about how to handle the situation. This reflects the early human struggle with understanding death and responsibility.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: What punishment did Qabil receive for murdering his brother?
opts: He was cursed and lived a life of regret and fear
He was turned into a crow
questions.explanation: Qabil was cursed by Allah for killing his brother Habil, which led to a life filled with regret and fear as a consequence of his grave sin. This punishment served as a warning about the seriousness of taking an innocent life.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: What did Iblis swear to do to the children of Adam after being granted respite?
opts: To beautify evil for them on earth and mislead them all except the sincere servants of Allah
To make them forget Allah completely
questions.explanation: Iblis vowed to deceive the children of Adam by making evil appear attractive and leading them astray, except for those who are truly devoted and sincere servants of Allah. This highlights his role as a tempter and the importance of sincere faith to resist his influence.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: What was Qabil's response after killing his brother and seeing the crow?
opts: He said "Woe is me! Was I unable to be like this crow and hide the body of my brother?"
He tried to bring Habil back to life
questions.explanation: After killing his brother, Qabil saw a crow burying another crow and realized he should have hidden his brother's body similarly. This moment reflects his regret and awareness of his wrongdoing, highlighting the early human understanding of guilt and responsibility.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: What function do mountains serve in relation to the earth, according to the Quranic verses cited by Ibn Kathir?
opts: They prevent the earth from shaking with the inhabitants
questions.explanation: Mountains act as stabilizers for the earth, preventing excessive shaking and maintaining balance, as described in the Quran and explained by Ibn Kathir. This reflects the Quranic view that mountains serve as pegs or anchors to keep the earth steady for its inhabitants.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: What did Iblis swear to do to the children of Adam?
opts: To mislead them all except the sincere servants of Allah
questions.explanation: Iblis vowed to misguide the descendants of Adam by tempting them away from Allah’s path, except for those who are truly devoted and sincere servants of Allah, who remain protected by His guidance and mercy.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: Who did not prostrate to Adam, and for what reason?
opts: Jibril, because he was higher in rank
Mika'il, because he was busy with rain
Iblis, out of arrogance and jealousy
Israfil, because he was preparing to blow the Trumpet
questions.explanation: Iblis refused to prostrate to Adam because of his arrogance and jealousy, believing he was superior as he was created from fire while Adam was made from clay. This act of disobedience led to his expulsion from God's mercy.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What covers the Sidrat al-Muntaha, according to the hadith of Ibn Mas'ud about the Prophet's ﷺ ascension?
questions.explanation: The Sidrat al-Muntaha is described as being covered by various magnificent elements, including its leaves, fruits, and flowers, symbolizing its immense beauty and significance in the heavens during the Prophet’s ﷺ ascension. This comprehensive coverage emphasizes its unique and exalted status.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What are the names of the two angels who question the dead in their graves?
questions.explanation: Munkar and Nakir are the two angels assigned to question the deceased in their graves about their faith and deeds. This questioning serves to test the person's belief and actions during their lifetime, as described in Islamic teachings and sources like Al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What happens to believing Jinn on the Day of Judgment?
questions.explanation: Believing Jinn, like righteous humans, are rewarded for their faith and good deeds by entering Paradise on the Day of Judgment, as they are accountable beings with free will who can choose to obey or disobey Allah.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: Can Jinn die?
opts: They die but are resurrected differently
questions.explanation: Jinn are created beings with a lifespan, so they do die like humans. Their existence is limited, and they will be resurrected on the Day of Judgment for accountability, similar to humans.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What does the Quranic phrase "la ya'suna Allaha ma amarahum wa yaf'aluna ma yu'marun" (they do not disobey Allah in what He commands them and do what they are commanded) indicate about angels?
opts: They have free will but choose to obey
They are compelled and have no choice
They are created with a nature that makes obedience effortless and disobedience impossible for them
They sometimes disobey but repent quickly
questions.explanation: This phrase highlights that angels are created with an inherent nature of complete obedience to Allah, meaning they do not possess free will to disobey or rebel, unlike humans and jinn. Their actions are always in perfect submission to Allah’s commands.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: What was Qabil's response after seeing the crow and burying his brother?
opts: He said, "Woe is me! Was I unable to be like this crow and hide the body of my brother?"
He tried to bring Habil back to life
questions.explanation: Qabil expressed regret and shame after seeing the crow bury its dead, realizing he had failed to properly cover his brother's body. This moment marks his awareness of the gravity of his actions and the importance of respecting the deceased.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: From what substance were the Jinn created, according to the Quran and hadith cited by Ibn Kathir?
questions.explanation: The Quran and hadith describe that jinn were created from "smokeless fire" (marj min nar), a subtle, invisible flame distinct from physical fire, highlighting their spiritual and unseen nature compared to humans, who were created from clay. This origin emphasizes their unique qualities and abilities.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: Did Allah grant Iblis's request for respite?
opts: Yes, until the Day of Judgment
Yes, until the appointed time (the Day of Judgment or the first blast)
questions.explanation: Allah granted Iblis respite until the Day of Judgment, allowing him to mislead humans within this worldly life. This delay serves as a test for human free will and faith, ending only at the final appointed time when ultimate justice is established.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: In the hadith about the Prophet's ﷺ journey to Ta'if, which angel came to him with an offer to destroy the people?
opts: The Angel of the Mountains (Malak al-Jibal)
questions.explanation: During the Prophet’s ﷺ journey to Ta'if, the Angel of the Mountains (Malak al-Jibal) appeared offering to crush the people between the mountains as punishment, but the Prophet ﷺ chose patience and prayed for their guidance instead, demonstrating mercy and forgiveness.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: According to some narrations, when Adam died, who performed his burial rites?
opts: His son Seth and the believing children
questions.explanation: When Adam passed away, it is narrated that his son Seth, along with the believing children, performed his burial rites, highlighting the early practice of honoring the deceased within the first human family. This reflects the importance of family responsibility and faith in Islamic tradition.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: Why did Qabil kill Habil?
opts: Because of a dispute over land
Because Habil refused to share his wealth
Because Qabil was jealous that Habil's sacrifice was accepted while his was not
Because their father Adam favored Habil
questions.explanation: Qabil killed Habil out of jealousy because Allah accepted Habil's sacrifice but rejected Qabil's. This event marks the first murder in human history, illustrating the consequences of envy and disobedience to Allah's commands.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: Why did Allah grant Iblis respite despite his disobedience?
opts: As a test for humanity and to fulfill divine wisdom
Because the angels interceded for him
questions.explanation: Allah granted Iblis respite to test human beings' faith and obedience, allowing them to choose between right and wrong, which fulfills divine wisdom by demonstrating free will and moral responsibility.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What is the first question asked by Munkar and Nakir in the grave?
opts: "How much wealth did you leave behind?"
questions.explanation: Munkar and Nakir ask "Who is your Lord?" first to test the deceased's faith and recognition of Allah as their Creator and Sustainer. This question establishes the foundation of belief in the Hereafter and the individual's relationship with God.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: What did Allah send to show Qabil how to bury his brother?
opts: A crow that scratched the ground to show him how to bury another dead crow
questions.explanation: Allah sent a crow that scratched the ground to demonstrate to Qabil how to bury his brother, teaching him the proper way to handle the deceased. This act symbolizes the beginning of burial practices in human history.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: What is the lifespan of the Jinn compared to humans?
opts: Longer than humans - some can live hundreds or thousands of years
questions.explanation: Jinn have a much longer lifespan than humans, often living for hundreds or even thousands of years. This extended lifespan allows them to witness many generations of humans and events throughout history, as described in Islamic teachings.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: According to the hadith about the "Lammah" (inclination) of the angel and the devil, what is the devil's inclination?
opts: To make promises of poverty and to command evil
questions.explanation: The devil's inclination is to make false promises of poverty and to command evil actions, aiming to mislead and harm humans. This contrasts with the angel's inclination, which encourages truth and righteousness.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: When Iblis asked Allah to be given respite until the Day of Judgment, what was Allah's response?
opts: He granted him respite until the Day of Judgment
He granted him respite until the appointed time (the Day of Judgment or the first blast)
He told him to ask again on the Day of Judgment
questions.explanation: Allah granted Iblis respite until the Day of Judgment, allowing him to continue his role in tempting humans and jinn until the appointed time when ultimate justice will be served. This shows Allah's wisdom in allowing free will and testing.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What is the distance between each heaven and the next, according to the hadith of Abu Dharr?
questions.explanation: According to the hadith narrated by Abu Dharr, the distance between each of the seven heavens is described as the distance one would travel in five hundred years. This illustrates the vastness and greatness of Allah’s creation beyond human comprehension.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Pen & The Tablet
questions.q: What words of repentance did Adam learn from his Lord, as mentioned in Surah al-Baqarah?
opts: "Rabbana zalamna anfusana wa in lam taghfir lana wa tarhamna lanakunanna min al-khasireen"
"La ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu min al-thalimeen"
questions.explanation: Adam was taught the words of repentance, "Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers" (Surah al-Baqarah 2:286), emphasizing sincere acknowledgment of sin and seeking Allah’s forgiveness and mercy.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What angel is appointed over the womb, according to authentic hadith?
opts: An angel specifically called the "Angel of the Womb" (Malak al-Rahim)
questions.explanation: An angel known as the "Angel of the Womb" (Malak al-Rahim) is appointed to protect and maintain the ties of kinship, ensuring family bonds are preserved, as mentioned in authentic hadith. This angel's role highlights the importance Islam places on family relationships.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: Which of the following characteristics is NOT true of angels according to Ibn Kathir's compilation?
opts: They do not eat or drink
They die before the Day of Judgment
They can disobey Allah if they choose
questions.explanation: Angels are created from light and are completely obedient to Allah; they do not possess free will to disobey Him. Unlike humans and jinn, angels always carry out Allah’s commands without error or rebellion.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What is the term used in the Quran for the angels of Hell who are harsh and severe?
questions.explanation: Al-Zabaniyah refers to the angels assigned to Hell who are described as harsh and severe in the Quran, specifically tasked with punishing the disbelievers and wrongdoers. Their role emphasizes the seriousness of divine justice in the afterlife.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: What is the meaning of "Daha" (دحا) in the verse "And the earth after that He spread it" (wal-arda ba'da dhalika dahaha)?
opts: He brought forth from it its water, its pasture, and made the mountains firm
He made it flat like a disk
questions.explanation: "Daha" (دحا) in this verse means that Allah caused the earth to bring forth its water, vegetation, and made the mountains stable, transforming it into a habitable and fertile place after its initial creation. This reflects the process of preparing the earth for life.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: Why did Allah take this covenant from humanity?
opts: To have evidence against them on the Day of Judgment
questions.explanation: Allah took the covenant from humanity to ensure they recognize Him as their Lord, so on the Day of Judgment, there will be clear evidence that they were aware of His existence and responsibility, making them accountable for their actions.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: According to Surah al-Isra' (17:64-65), what resources did Allah permit Iblis to use in his attempts to mislead humanity?
opts: His voice, his cavalry and infantry, sharing in wealth and children, and making promises
questions.explanation: Allah allowed Iblis to use his voice to whisper and deceive, along with his forces like cavalry and infantry, to influence humans through wealth, children, and false promises, as mentioned in Surah al-Isra' (17:64-65), highlighting the means of temptation granted to him to test human faith.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: What were the occupations of Habil (Abel) and Qabil (Cain)?
opts: Habil was a farmer, Qabil was a shepherd
Habil was a shepherd, Qabil was a farmer
questions.explanation: Habil (Abel) was a shepherd who tended animals, while Qabil (Cain) was a farmer who cultivated the land. Their different occupations are mentioned in Islamic tradition to highlight their distinct roles and the story of their offerings to Allah.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: After creating the Pen, what command did Allah give it?
opts: To write the destinies of all creation until the Day of Judgment
To measure the heavens and earth
To record the names of the prophets
questions.explanation: After creating the Pen, Allah commanded it to write everything that will happen, including the destinies of all creatures until the Day of Judgment. This shows Allah’s knowledge and control over all events from the beginning to the end of time.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: Are the Jinn visible to humans in their natural form?
opts: No, not usually - they can see us but we cannot see them
questions.explanation: Jinn are created from smokeless fire and exist in a different realm, making them generally invisible to human eyes. While jinn can see humans, humans cannot see jinn unless they choose to reveal themselves or take a visible form.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: According to the hadith about the beginning of the day, what do the angel and the devil say when a person wakes up?
opts: The angel says "Begin with good" and the devil says "Begin with evil"
The angel says "Pray" and the devil says "Sleep more"
The angel says "Remember Allah" and the devil says "Forget Him"
The angel says "Be generous" and the devil says "Be stingy"
questions.explanation: When a person wakes up, the angel encourages starting the day with good deeds, promoting righteousness, while the devil tempts them toward evil actions. This reflects the ongoing spiritual struggle between guidance and temptation in a believer's life.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What is the origin of the word "Jinn"?
questions.explanation: The word "Jinn" originates from the Arabic root "Ijtinan," which means hidden or concealed, reflecting their unseen nature. This emphasizes that jinn are invisible beings created by Allah, distinct from humans and angels.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: What was the first form of idolatry that appeared among humanity?
opts: Worship of images of righteous people who had died (Wadd, Suwa', Yaghuth, Ya'uq, Nasr)
Worship of the sun and moon
questions.explanation: The first form of idolatry involved worshipping images of righteous people who had passed away, such as Wadd, Suwa', Yaghuth, Ya'uq, and Nasr, as a way to seek their intercession or blessings, which deviated from pure monotheism taught by the prophets.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: According to Ibn Kathir's description, what is the relationship between the Throne (`Arsh) and the Kursi (Footstool)?
opts: They are the same thing with two different names
The Kursi is above the Throne and contains it
The Throne is above the Kursi and is greater in size than it
The Kursi is inside the Throne and carries it
questions.explanation: Ibn Kathir explains that the Throne (`Arsh) is positioned above the Kursi (Footstool) and is much larger in size, symbolizing Allah's supreme authority and grandeur. The Kursi supports the Throne, highlighting a hierarchy in creation and divine power.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: Was Iblis an angel or a jinn?
questions.explanation: Iblis was a jinn, not an angel, because angels are created from light and always obey Allah, while jinn are created from smokeless fire and have free will, allowing Iblis to disobey Allah's command to bow to Adam.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: According to Abu Musa al-Ash'ari's narration cited by `Abd al-Razzaq, what did Allah teach Adam when he was sent down to earth?
opts: The craft of everything, and he was given provisions from Paradise's fruits, though earthly ones change
questions.explanation: When Adam was sent down to earth, Allah taught him the craft of everything, meaning the knowledge and skills needed for life, and provided him with fruits from Paradise as provisions, highlighting the difference between eternal heavenly sustenance and the changing, perishable nature of earthly provisions.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: How long did Iblis worship Allah with the angels?
opts: A period known only to Allah - narrations vary and are from Isra'iliyyat
questions.explanation: The exact duration of Iblis's worship with the angels is not specified in authentic Islamic texts and varies in Isra'iliyyat narrations, which are not considered reliable. Only Allah knows the true length of this period, emphasizing the limits of human knowledge regarding unseen matters.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Adam's Descent & Early Humanity
questions.q: What was the nature of their sacrifices?
opts: Qabil offered his worst crops, Habil offered his best animal
Qabil offered his best, Habil offered his worst
questions.explanation: Qabil's sacrifice consisted of his worst crops, showing a lack of sincerity, while Habil offered his best animal, demonstrating true devotion. This difference in the quality of their offerings reflected their intentions and was why Habil's sacrifice was accepted by Allah.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What did Allah say to Adam after he ate from the tree?
opts: "Did I not forbid you from that tree and tell you that Satan is your clear enemy?"
questions.explanation: Allah reminded Adam that He had explicitly forbidden him from eating from the tree and warned that Satan is a clear enemy who deceives. This highlights the importance of obedience to Allah's commands and awareness of Satan's role in leading humans astray.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: How many earths did Allah create, according to Surah al-Talaq?
questions.explanation: Surah al-Talaq (65:12) mentions that Allah created seven heavens and seven earths, indicating multiple layers or realms of creation. This emphasizes the vastness and complexity of Allah’s creation beyond just the single earth we inhabit.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What did Allah create the heavens from, according to Surah Fussilat?
questions.explanation: According to Surah Fussilat (41:11), Allah created the heavens from "smoke" (dukhan), indicating a primordial, gaseous state before forming the structured universe. This highlights the initial formlessness from which the heavens were fashioned.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: What types of Jinn exist according to the narrations?
opts: Three types: those with wings that fly, those that are snakes and dogs, and those that reside and travel
Seven types corresponding to the seven earths
questions.explanation: According to narrations, jinn are categorized into three types: winged jinn that can fly, jinn that take the form of snakes and dogs, and jinn that live among humans and travel. This classification highlights their diverse forms and abilities as described in Islamic tradition.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a description of the Throne mentioned by Ibn Kathir?
opts: It is vast and immense beyond imagination
questions.explanation: Ibn Kathir describes the Throne as immense and majestic but does not mention it being red in color. The Throne symbolizes Allah’s supreme authority and greatness, and its descriptions focus on its vastness and grandeur rather than specific colors.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: Which angel is entrusted with provision, rain, and sustenance?
questions.explanation: Mika'il (Michael) is the angel responsible for providing sustenance, including rain and nourishment, to all creatures. This role highlights his duty in maintaining the natural order and ensuring that God's creation receives what it needs to survive.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: After Adam demonstrated his knowledge of the names, what did Allah command the angels to do?
questions.explanation: Allah commanded the angels to prostrate to Adam as a sign of respect and acknowledgment of his special status and knowledge, which demonstrated his superiority over the angels in this aspect. This act symbolized Adam's honored position as the first human and Allah's vicegerent on Earth.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: Where did Adam descend to earth, according to various narrations cited by Ibn Kathir?
opts: India (Hind), a land called Dahna between Makkah and Taif, or on Safa and Marwa
questions.explanation: According to Ibn Kathir, narrations differ on where Adam descended to earth, mentioning locations such as India (Hind), a place called Dahna between Makkah and Taif, or the hills of Safa and Marwa near Makkah. These variations reflect different traditions within Islamic scholarship.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: Which angel is entrusted with taking the souls of humans at death?
questions.explanation: Azra'il, also known as Malak al-Mawt (Angel of Death), is the angel responsible for taking the souls of humans at the time of death, as mentioned in Islamic teachings. This role is specific to him, distinguishing him from other angels with different duties.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: How long did it take Allah to create the heavens and the earth, as repeatedly mentioned in the Quran?
questions.explanation: Allah created the heavens and the earth in six days, as stated in multiple Quranic verses (e.g., Surah Al-A'raf 7:54). This period signifies stages of creation, emphasizing Allah’s power and control over time and existence.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: How does Ibn Kathir reconcile the verse that says the earth was created in two days (Surah Fussilat) with the hadith that spreads creation over specific days?
opts: He explains that the "two days" refer to the Sunday and Monday mentioned in the hadith, and the "four days" for provisions include Tuesday through Friday
He says the hadith is weak and cannot be used
He says the Quranic days are not the same as earthly days
questions.explanation: Ibn Kathir explains that the "two days" in Surah Fussilat correspond to Sunday and Monday from the hadith, during which the earth was created, while the "four days" mentioned for spreading provisions refer to Tuesday through Friday, harmonizing the Quranic verse with the prophetic narration.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What happens to the disbelieving or hypocritical soul when it fails to answer correctly?
opts: The angels beat him with iron hammers
His grave constricts until his ribs interlace
A door to Hell is opened for him
questions.explanation: When a disbelieving or hypocritical soul fails to answer correctly, it faces punishment and is rejected by the angels, reflecting its failure to meet the divine test. This emphasizes the seriousness of faith and accountability in the afterlife.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What is the wisdom behind Allah creating the universe in six days when He could have created it in an instant with the word "Be"?
opts: To teach angels the process of creation
To teach mankind patience and to show that matters unfold gradually with wisdom
Because the materials needed time to form
questions.explanation: Allah created the universe in six days to demonstrate the importance of patience and gradual progress, teaching humans that significant outcomes develop step by step with divine wisdom, rather than instantaneously. This process reflects order and purpose in creation.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: When were the Jinn created in relation to humans?
opts: At the same time as Adam
After the angels but before Adam
questions.explanation: Jinn were created before Adam, as mentioned in Islamic teachings, where Allah created them from smokeless fire prior to creating humans from clay. This order highlights the distinct nature and origin of jinn compared to humans.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What happens to the believing soul when it answers correctly that Allah is his Lord, Islam is his religion, and Muhammad ﷺ is his prophet?
opts: The angels congratulate him and leave
His grave is expanded for him as far as the eye can see, and a door to Paradise is opened
He is immediately taken to Paradise
He sleeps until the Day of Judgment
questions.explanation: When a believing soul affirms faith in Allah, Islam, and Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, it experiences comfort and ease in the grave, symbolized by its expansion, and is granted hope by the opening of a door to Paradise, reflecting the soul’s acceptance and reward in the afterlife.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What was the practice of Adam's children regarding marriage?
opts: A son from one pregnancy would marry a daughter from a different pregnancy (twins did not marry each other)
questions.explanation: Adam's children married siblings from different pregnancies to avoid incest, as twins or siblings from the same pregnancy did not marry each other. This practice ensured lawful and ethical unions according to early human social norms.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What are the names of some of Iblis's offspring mentioned in some narrations?
opts: Awar, Zalambur, etc. - but these are from Isra'iliyyat
They are not named in authentic sources
questions.explanation: Some narrations mention Iblis's offspring like Awar and Zalambur, but these names come from Isra'iliyyat—stories of Jewish or Christian origin not confirmed by authentic Islamic sources—so they are not part of orthodox Islamic teachings.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What did Allah create on Thursday, according to the same hadith?
