questions.explanation: On Thursday, Allah created all the moving creatures (dawabb), which include animals and other living beings that move. This highlights the order of creation, where living creatures were created after the heavens, earth, and other elements.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Pen & The Tablet
questions.q: According to a different authentic narration, what existed before the Throne?
questions.explanation: Before the creation of the Throne, there existed only water, as mentioned in authentic narrations. This highlights the primordial state of creation, where water was the first element from which Allah began creating the universe.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: According to a hadith, what do the angels say when a believer says "Alhamdulillah" after sneezing?
opts: The angels remain silent, only humans respond
questions.explanation: When a believer says "Alhamdulillah" after sneezing, the angels respond by saying "YarhamukAllah," meaning "May Allah have mercy on you." This reflects the Islamic etiquette of responding with mercy and blessings, emphasizing the spiritual connection between believers and angels in daily acts.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: In the hadith of Abu Hurairah about creation on specific days, what did Allah create on Wednesday?
questions.explanation: On Wednesday, Allah created the light, which is considered the first creation and the source of all illumination in the universe. This highlights the importance of light as a fundamental element in the order of creation.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Pen & The Tablet
questions.q: What does this hadith teach about the gravity of murder?
opts: Each murderer only bears their own sin
The one who introduces an evil practice bears the sin of all who follow it without diminishing their sins
questions.explanation: This hadith emphasizes that initiating a sinful act, like murder, causes the originator to carry the burden of all subsequent sinners who imitate it, in addition to their own sins, highlighting the severe responsibility and gravity of starting such evil practices.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: How many earths did Allah create, according to the Quranic verse cited by Ibn Kathir?
opts: Four earths corresponding to the four directions
questions.explanation: The Quran mentions that Allah created seven earths, indicating multiple layers or realms of the earth, as referenced in Surah At-Talaq (65:12). Ibn Kathir explains this to show the vastness and complexity of Allah’s creation beyond just the single earth we live on.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: According to some narrations, what items came down with Adam from Paradise?
opts: A tent, a staff, and a sword
The Black Stone, a handful of Paradise's leaves, and knowledge of all trades
questions.explanation: Adam ﷺ descended from Paradise carrying the Black Stone, a handful of Paradise's leaves, and knowledge of all trades, symbolizing divine blessings and guidance for humanity. These items represent spiritual connection, healing, and practical skills essential for human life on Earth.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What is the name of the special tree at the boundary of the seventh heaven, which was mentioned in the context of the Prophet's ﷺ ascension?
opts: The Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary (Sidrat al-Muntaha)
questions.explanation: The Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary (Sidrat al-Muntaha) is a magnificent tree at the edge of the seventh heaven, marking the limit beyond which no creation can pass. It was mentioned during the Prophet’s ﷺ ascension (Isra and Mi'raj) as a symbol of divine proximity and the boundary of knowledge.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: What is the "cavalry and infantry" of Iblis, as explained by the commentators?
opts: Actual horses and soldiers from the jinn
All those who obey him and spread evil, whether riding or walking
The demons who ride on animals and those who walk on the ground
The thoughts that come to humans when they are mounted or walking
questions.explanation: The "cavalry and infantry" of Iblis symbolize all his followers who carry out his commands and spread evil, regardless of their mode of movement. This metaphor highlights that both active and passive agents contribute to his influence in the world.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: From what stages of materials was Adam created, as detailed in the Quranic verses cited by Ibn Kathir?
opts: From clay, then sticky mud (teen laazib), then dried clay (salsal) like pottery, then breathed into
From dust blown by the wind
From a single drop of fluid
questions.explanation: Adam was created in stages starting from clay, which then became sticky mud, followed by dried clay resembling pottery, before Allah breathed life into him. This process highlights the gradual formation of human creation as described in the Quran and explained by Ibn Kathir.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: Do the Jinn have different religions and sects like humans?
opts: No, they are all one religion
Yes, among them are Muslims, Jews, Christians, and disbelievers
questions.explanation: Jinn, like humans, possess free will and can choose different beliefs, resulting in various religions and sects among them, including Islam, Judaism, Christianity, and disbelief. This diversity is mentioned in the Quran and Islamic teachings, highlighting their moral responsibility similar to humans.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: According to the hadith of Abu Dharr, what fills the space of four fingers in the heavens?
questions.explanation: In the hadith of Abu Dharr, the space of four fingers in the heavens is filled with angels prostrating to Allah, demonstrating their continuous worship and submission. This illustrates the vast number of angels and their devotion in the unseen realms.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: How many wings did the Prophet ﷺ see Jibril have when he observed him in his original form?
questions.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ saw Jibril in his original form with six hundred wings, as described in authentic narrations. This immense number of wings signifies the angel's grandeur and the vastness of Allah's creation beyond human perception.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: How did Iblis exploit this practice?
opts: He encouraged people to worship the images after the generation that made them passed away
He told people the images were gods
questions.explanation: Iblis exploited the practice of venerating images by encouraging people to continue worshipping these idols even after the original creators had died, diverting them from the worship of Allah and leading them into shirk (associating partners with Allah).

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: Where did the snake descend to earth, according to some narrations?
questions.explanation: According to some narrations in Islamic tradition, the snake descended to earth in Isfahan, a city in Persia. This detail highlights specific geographic references used in historical texts like Al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya to explain early events in creation stories.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: Where was the Throne of Allah before the creation of the heavens and earth?
questions.explanation: Before creating the heavens and the earth, Allah's Throne was established over the water, as mentioned in Islamic texts like Al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya. This signifies the Throne's pre-existence and Allah's supreme authority over all creation from the very beginning.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: How many angels are the keepers (guards) of Hellfire, according to Surah al-Muddaththir (74:30-31)?
questions.explanation: Surah al-Muddaththir (74:30-31) mentions that nineteen angels are appointed as keepers of Hellfire, emphasizing their role in guarding and controlling the punishment. This specific number highlights the severity and order established by Allah in managing Hell.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: According to Ibn Kathir's explanation, what is the significance of the verse "And He is the Lord of the Mighty Throne"?
opts: It emphasizes the greatness of the Throne
It means Allah is the Owner and Possessor of the Throne, which is the greatest of all creations
It means Allah sits on the Throne
It limits Allah to the Throne
questions.explanation: This verse emphasizes Allah's absolute sovereignty and authority, highlighting that the Throne is the greatest creation, symbolizing His supreme power and dominion over all existence. It signifies that Allah alone owns and controls everything in the universe.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: Which angel is entrusted with provision and rain?
questions.explanation: Mika'il is the angel responsible for providing sustenance to creatures, including sending rain and managing natural provisions. This role is mentioned in Islamic tradition, highlighting his duty to ensure the earth's fertility and the well-being of living beings.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What is the significance of this covenant for human accountability?
opts: No one can claim ignorance of Allah's existence and Lordship on the Day of Judgment
Only those who remember the covenant are responsible
The covenant was only for Adam's generation
questions.explanation: This covenant refers to the primordial testimony where all human souls acknowledged Allah's Lordship before earthly life, ensuring that on the Day of Judgment, no one can deny or claim ignorance of Allah's existence and authority, establishing universal human accountability.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: Where did Hawwa (Eve) descend to earth, according to these narrations?
questions.explanation: According to narrations in Al-Bidaya wal-Nihaya, Hawwa (Eve) descended to earth in Jeddah, near Mecca. This location is significant in Islamic tradition as the place where the first humans began their life on earth.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: According to some narrations mentioned by Ibn Kathir, who ate from the tree first?
opts: Iblis made them both eat at the same moment
questions.explanation: Hawwa (Eve) is mentioned in some narrations by Ibn Kathir as the first to eat from the forbidden tree, which led to both her and Adam’s slip. This highlights the initial human error before seeking Allah’s forgiveness and repentance.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: Does Iblis have offspring?
opts: Yes, he has children and descendants who help him
questions.explanation: Iblis is described in Islamic tradition as having offspring, often referred to as devils or shayatin, who assist him in leading humans astray. These descendants work under his command to spread evil and temptation in the world.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: According to Ibn Abbas, how long did Adam remain in Paradise before being sent down?
opts: Only between the afternoon prayer (`Asr) and sunset
questions.explanation: Ibn Abbas explains that Adam ﷺ stayed in Paradise for a very short time—from the afternoon prayer (`Asr) until sunset—highlighting the immediacy of his descent to Earth and the beginning of human life here. This emphasizes the swift transition from creation to earthly existence.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: According to Surah al-Tahrim (66:6), what are the characteristics of the angels of Hell?
opts: They are harsh, severe, and do not disobey Allah in what He commands them
They are made of fire like the inhabitants
questions.explanation: The angels of Hell are described as harsh and severe because they carry out Allah’s punishment without leniency. They strictly obey Allah’s commands without question, ensuring justice is fulfilled precisely as ordained in Surah al-Tahrim (66:6).

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: When Allah created the Pen, what command did He give it?
opts: To write the destinies of all creation until the Day of Judgment
To measure the distances between the heavens
To record the names of the prophets
questions.explanation: When Allah created the Pen, He commanded it to write everything that would happen until the Day of Judgment, including the destinies of all creatures. This shows Allah's complete knowledge and control over all events in the universe.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What was the immediate consequence for Adam and Hawwa after eating from the tree?
opts: Their nakedness became apparent to them, and they began to cover themselves with leaves from Paradise
They were immediately removed from Paradise
questions.explanation: After eating from the tree, Adam and Hawwa became aware of their nakedness, symbolizing the loss of their initial innocence, and they covered themselves with leaves, reflecting their newfound sense of shame and awareness of disobedience.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: Why did each of them offer a sacrifice to Allah?
opts: To fulfill a command to give charity
To seek nearness to Allah, possibly as part of their religious practice
questions.explanation: Each offered a sacrifice to Allah to express their devotion and seek closeness to Him, demonstrating submission and gratitude as part of their worship, which is a common practice in many prophetic traditions.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What is the name of the chief angel responsible for Hellfire?
questions.explanation: Malik is the chief angel appointed to guard Hellfire, as mentioned in Islamic teachings. He oversees the punishment of the inhabitants of Hell and ensures that the commands related to Hellfire are carried out.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: According to the authentic hadith, what is the ruling regarding a soul that is killed unjustly?
opts: The killer alone bears the sin
The killer and his tribe bear the sin
The first son of Adam (Qabil) bears a portion of the sin of every murder because he was the first to establish the precedent of murder
Adam bears the sin because of his original sin
questions.explanation: When a soul is killed unjustly, the sin extends beyond the immediate murderer; Qabil, Adam’s first son, carries part of this sin because he set the initial example of murder, establishing a grave precedent in human history as explained in authentic hadith and Islamic teachings.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: What did Allah send to show Qabil how to bury his brother's body?
opts: A crow that scratched the ground to show him how to bury another dead crow
questions.explanation: Allah sent a crow that scratched the ground to demonstrate to Qabil how to bury his brother's body, teaching him the proper way to handle the deceased. This act symbolizes the beginning of burial practices in human history.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: According to various narrations compiled by Ibn Kathir, where did Adam (peace be upon him) descend to earth?
opts: India (Hind), a land called Dahna between Makkah and Taif, or on the mountains of Safa and Marwa
questions.explanation: Ibn Kathir's narrations mention different locations for Adam's descent, including India (Hind), Dahna between Makkah and Taif, or the mountains of Safa and Marwa, reflecting varied traditions rather than a single definitive place. These narrations highlight the diversity of early Islamic historical accounts.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What oath did Iblis swear to convince Adam and Hawwa?
opts: "I swear by Allah that I am your advisor"
"Indeed, I am to you from among the sincere advisors"
"I swear I am telling the truth"
questions.explanation: Iblis swore by claiming he was a sincere advisor to gain Adam and Hawwa's trust, intending to mislead them. This false oath was a tactic to deceive and cause them to disobey Allah.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What does the Angel of the Womb say when the fetus is forty days old?
opts: "O Lord, is it male or female?"
"O Lord, what is its provision, its lifespan, and is it wretched or blessed?"
"O Lord, when will it be born?"
"O Lord, who will be its parents?"
questions.explanation: When the fetus is forty days old, the Angel of the Womb asks Allah about the child's provision, lifespan, and destiny, highlighting that these aspects of life are divinely decreed even before birth, emphasizing Allah's control over human fate from the earliest stages of existence.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: Which angels are described as being "with Allah" and never getting tired of worshipping Him?
questions.explanation: Al-Muqarrabun are angels who are brought near to Allah’s presence and continuously worship Him without fatigue, as described in the Quran. Their closeness to Allah signifies their exalted status and constant devotion.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: What is the significance of the phrase "Bismillah majraha wa mursaha" (In the name of Allah is its course and its anchorage)?
opts: It teaches that everything begins and ends with the name of Allah
It was the name of the ship
It is a prayer for safety
questions.explanation: The phrase "Bismillah majraha wa mursaha" emphasizes that all actions and events, from their start to their completion, occur by Allah’s will and power, highlighting His control over every process and outcome in the universe.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What do the angels who carry the Throne constantly say, according to authentic hadith?
opts: "Glory be to You, O Allah, and praise be to You, and forgiveness be to You"
"Holy, Holy, Holy is the Lord of Hosts"
questions.explanation: The angels who carry the Throne continuously glorify Allah by saying, "Glory be to You, O Allah, and praise be to You, and forgiveness be to You," as this reflects their constant worship and acknowledgment of Allah’s majesty and mercy, as mentioned in authentic hadith.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Pen & The Tablet
questions.q: In the hadith about the creation of the Pen, what did the Pen write first?
opts: The destinies of all creation until the Hour
questions.explanation: The Pen first wrote the destinies of all creation until the Hour, meaning it recorded everything that will happen in the universe, including the lifespan and fate of all beings, as part of Allah’s divine knowledge and decree before creation began.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: True/False: The Throne (`Arsh) is above the Kursi (Footstool), and it is greater than it.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: Scenario: Someone says 'Ayat al-Kursi means Allah is contained within the Kursi.' What is the best response?
opts: Incorrect—Ayat al-Kursi affirms Allah’s sovereignty; His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth, and Allah is not contained by creation
It is unknown and should not be discussed
Ayat al-Kursi only describes the seventh heaven

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: True/False: Angels were created from light (nur).

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels · The Four Great Angels
questions.q: Which of the following is entrusted with blowing the Trumpet (Sur) on the Day of Resurrection?

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Jinn & Iblis
questions.q: Scenario: A learner says 'Iblis was an angel.' Which statement best matches the Qur’an’s wording?
opts: Correct—he was one of the angels created from light
Incorrect—he was from the jinn and disobeyed the command of his Lord
He was a human who became a jinn

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What is the Bayt al-Ma'mur (the Much-Frequented House) as mentioned in authentic narrations?
opts: A house in the first heaven where angels gather
A house in the seventh heaven visited by 70,000 angels daily who never return
A mountain on earth where angels descend

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Pen & The Tablet
questions.q: True/False: The first thing Allah created was the Pen (Al-Qalam).

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Pen & The Tablet
questions.q: After creating the Pen, what was it commanded to do?
opts: Write the destinies of all creation until the Hour
Write only the names of prophets

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Heavens & Earth
questions.q: True/False: The Qur’an repeatedly mentions that the heavens and the earth were created in six days.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Heavens & Earth
questions.q: According to Surah Fussilat, from what were the heavens fashioned at the beginning?

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Heavens & Earth
questions.q: Scenario: A student claims 'The seven heavens are the same as the seven earths.' Which response is most accurate?
opts: Incorrect—the heavens are above, and the earths are below, each in layers with separation mentioned in narrations
Only philosophers discuss this; there is no revelation about it

questions.topic: La ilaha illa Allah
questions.q: According to Dr. Sallabi, what is the FIRST word through which a person enters Islam and ascends in the stages of servitude?
opts: A. Bismillah – beginning all deeds in Allah's name
B. 'There is no god but Allah, Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah' – the declaration of monotheism
C. The Shahada of prayer – Allahu Akbar
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states: 'The first word through which a person enters the gateway of Islam, reaches the levels of monotheism, and ascends in the stages of servitude, is the phrase: There is no god but Allah, Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah.' By this, the servant acknowledges Allah alone as deserving of worship.

questions.topic: La ilaha illa Allah
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi cites the analogy of Wahb ibn Munabbih who was asked: 'Is La ilaha illa Allah the key to Paradise?' What was his answer?
opts: A. Yes, it is the only key and nothing more is needed
B. Yes, but a key has teeth — if the key has teeth (conditions), it opens; otherwise it will not
C. No, because deeds must accompany the statement
D. It depends on whether it is said with the tongue or the heart
questions.explanation: Wahb ibn Munabbih said: 'Yes, but a key has teeth; if you bring a key with teeth, it will open for you, otherwise it will not.' These teeth are the SEVEN conditions of La ilaha illa Allah. Knowing the words alone is not enough — the conditions must be fulfilled.

questions.topic: La ilaha illa Allah – Conditions
questions.q: What are the SEVEN conditions of La ilaha illa Allah according to scholars, as cited by Dr. Sallabi?
opts: A. Faith, Prayer, Fasting, Zakat, Hajj, Jihad, Remembrance
B. Knowledge, Certainty, Acceptance, Submission, Truthfulness, Sincerity, Love
C. Belief, Action, Intention, Consistency, Patience, Gratitude, Trust
D. Tawhid, Prophethood, Angels, Books, Day of Judgment, Qadr, Resurrection
questions.explanation: The 7 conditions are: (1) Knowledge — negating ignorance; (2) Certainty — negating doubt; (3) Acceptance with heart and tongue; (4) Submission — negating abandonment; (5) Truthfulness — negating falsehood/hypocrisy; (6) Sincerity — negating shirk in intention; (7) Love — for Allah and hatred of what contradicts it.

questions.topic: La ilaha illa Allah – Virtues
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi cites the famous hadith of the 'card' (Bitaqah) about the Day of Judgment. What does this hadith prove about La ilaha illa Allah?
opts: A. That scrolls of good deeds outweigh sins always
B. That 99 scrolls of sins were outweighed by one small card bearing 'La ilaha illa Allah, Muhammad is His servant and Messenger' — nothing is heavier than the name of Allah
C. That sins are forgiven if a person says La ilaha illa Allah once before death
D. That the testimony must be on a written record for it to be accepted on the Day of Judgment
questions.explanation: The hadith narrated by Al-Tirmidhi: Allah will bring out a card bearing 'I testify La ilaha illa Allah and that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger.' The man will say: 'What good is this card next to these 99 scrolls [of sins]?' Allah will say: 'You shall not be wronged.' The scrolls will be placed on one pan and the card on the other — the scrolls will be light, and the card will be heavy. 'Nothing is heavier than the name of Allah.'

questions.topic: La ilaha illa Allah – Effects
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi lists 9 effects of believing in La ilaha illa Allah on a person's life. Which of these is listed as effect number 7 — about bravery?
opts: A. The believer avoids all sins due to consciousness of Allah
B. Belief removes two causes of cowardice: love of worldly things, and fear that someone other than Allah can cause death — making the believer the bravest person
C. The believer becomes humble without humiliation and elevated without arrogance
D. The believer does not fall into despair because Allah holds the treasures of heaven and earth
questions.explanation: Effect 7: 'This phrase encourages a person and fills their heart with bravery, because cowardice has two causes: love for oneself/wealth/family AND belief that someone other than Allah can cause death. La ilaha illa Allah removes both fears. Therefore, there is no one braver than the one who believes in Allah — unshaken by armies, drawn swords, flying bullets, or raining bombs.'

questions.topic: Evidence for Creator's Existence
questions.q: How does the Holy Qur'an primarily approach proving Allah's existence, according to Dr. Sallabi?
opts: A. Through philosophical debate and logical syllogisms
B. Not through explicit rational debate, but through signs in the horizons and within oneself — because the Qur'an treats the universe as an exhibition of faith
C. By debating atheists and polytheists with direct argumentation in every surah
D. By quoting scientific proofs from chemistry and physics
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi notes: 'The Holy Qur'an did not engage in an explicit discussion with those who deny the Creator... The Holy Qur'an treats the entire universe as an exhibition of signs of faith and its evidence, a page open to the senses and hearts.' The proofs in Qur'an were mostly used to establish other matters like Oneness, prophethood, and resurrection.

questions.topic: Evidence of Creation (Proof 1)
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi quotes the verse: {Or were they created by nothing, or were they the creators of themselves?} [At-Tur: 35]. Imam Abu Hanifa used a similar argument with atheists. What was his illustration?
opts: A. He asked them to look at a book — can it write itself?
B. He described a ship sailing in a turbulent sea without a captain and asked if this is reasonable — just as the world cannot exist without a Creator
C. He asked them to consider how seeds grow into trees without any planter
D. He challenged them to produce something from nothing to prove their claim
questions.explanation: Imam Abu Hanifa asked: 'What do you say about a ship loaded with goods, sailing smoothly through turbulent waves without a captain or guide — is this reasonable?' They replied: 'No.' He said: 'Glory be to Allah! If a ship cannot sail without a captain, how can this vast world with all its conditions exist without a Creator or Sustainer?' They all wept and repented.

questions.topic: Evidence of Innate Nature (Proof 2)
questions.q: What does the Qur'anic term 'FITRAH' mean in the verse: {[Adhere to] the fitrah of Allah upon which He has created all people} [Ar-Rum: 30], according to Dr. Sallabi?
opts: A. The natural physical form Allah gave humans
B. Islam and the inherent disposition toward monotheism that Allah has created all people upon
C. The covenant that was taken from all humans before creation
D. The conscience that guides humans toward good and away from evil
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states: 'The term fitrah here refers to Islam. Allah Almighty has created people with an inherent disposition toward Islam and monotheism.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Every child is born with a true faith of Islam and his parents convert him to Judaism, Christianity, or Magianism, as an animal delivers a perfect baby animal.'

questions.topic: Evidence from the Horizons (Proof 3)
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi discusses 6 scientific miracles as signs from the horizons. Which of the following is one of them?
opts: A. The Qur'an describes the Earth as flat, matching ancient geography
B. The Qur'an indicated oxygen decreases at high altitudes [Al-An'am: 125], the movement of stars in orbits [Ya-Sin: 40], and a barrier between two salty seas discovered in 1962 [Ar-Rahman: 19-22]
C. The Qur'an predicted the invention of the internet and telecommunications
D. The Qur'an described the Earth revolving around the sun before Galileo
questions.explanation: The 6 scientific miracles cited include: (1) Decrease of oxygen at high altitudes; (2) Stars and planets moving in orbits; (3) Earth and mountains rotating; (4) A barrier between two salty seas (discovered 1962); (5) Earth quivering and swelling with rain; (6) The spider's web being the weakest of homes — all confirmed by modern science.

questions.topic: Evidence of Guidance (Proof 5)
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi uses the bee as an example of Allah's guidance (Proof 5). What UNIQUE fact does he mention about the queen bee — which mirrors how Allah guides His creation?
opts: A. The queen bee is the only bee that can sting multiple times
B. The queen produces all the honey alone while other bees merely collect pollen
C. The queen manages the hive like a king managing subjects — it commands, they obey; it stands at the entrance ensuring no bee crowds another, one by one without crowding
D. The queen bee lives 10 times longer than other bees due to a special protein
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi writes: 'The bee has a queen called 'queen bee' without whom there is no departure, return, work, or pasture... When bees return to their homes, the queen stands at the entrance ensuring no bee crowds another — they pass one by one without crowding, just as a leader does when his army reaches a narrow crossing.' Allah guided them: {Follow the ways of your Lord laid down for you} [An-Nahl: 68-69].

questions.topic: Ayat al-Kursi
questions.q: Why does Dr. Sallabi describe Ayat al-Kursi as 'the greatest verse in the Book of Allah'?
opts: A. Because it is the longest verse in the Qur'an
B. Because everything in it pertains to the exalted divine essence, speaking of His Lordship, divinity, names and attributes of perfection, knowledge, and great authority
C. Because it is the most frequently recited verse in daily prayers
D. Because the Prophet ﷺ said it is equal to one-third of the Qur'an
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi says: 'Ayat al-Kursi is considered the best verse in the Book of Allah, as everything in it pertains to the exalted divine essence, speaking of His Lordship, divinity, and His names and attributes that indicate the perfection of His essence, knowledge, power, and great authority.' It fills the heart with awe of Allah's majesty.

questions.topic: Ayat al-Kursi
questions.q: In explaining Ayat al-Kursi's phrase: {His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth}, Dr. Sallabi cites Ibn Abbas's interpretation. What did Ibn Abbas say the Kursi refers to?
opts: A. The physical throne of Allah above the seven heavens
B. Allah's knowledge — indicating the vastness of His dominion and knowledge
C. The footstool placed beneath the Great Throne
D. The boundary between the heavens and the earth
questions.explanation: Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) interpreted {His Kursi} as His knowledge. Dr. Sallabi notes: 'Interpreting it as a symbol of His authority aligns with the verse {and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge}. It is a metaphor for the greatness of His power, the extent of His will, the vastness of His knowledge.' The Hadith also states: 'The Kursi compared to the Throne is like a ring thrown in a vast desert.'

questions.topic: Ayat al-Kursi
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi cites the comparison between the heavens/earth, the Kursi, and the Throne. What is the exact ratio described?
opts: A. The Earth is to the solar system as the Kursi is to the Throne
B. The seven heavens in the Kursi are like a ring in a vast desert — and the Kursi compared to the Throne is also like a ring compared to a vast desert
C. The Kursi is 7 times the size of the heavens, and the Throne is 70 times the size of the Kursi
D. The sun is to the Milky Way as the Kursi is to the heavens
questions.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ told Abu Dharr: 'O Abu Dharr, the heavens in comparison to the Kursi are like a ring thrown in a vast desert, and the superiority of the Throne over the Kursi is like the superiority of the desert over the ring.' If a small iron ring — no more than 3 inches — is placed in a vast desert like the Sahara, this is the ratio of the seven heavens to the Kursi, and similarly Kursi to Throne.

questions.topic: Beginning of Creation
questions.q: The Prophet ﷺ answered a delegation from Yemen with four foundational statements about the beginning of creation. What were these four matters?
opts: A. Light was created first; then water; then earth; then sky
B. (1) Allah existed and there was nothing else; (2) His Throne was on water; (3) He wrote all things in the Preserved Tablet; (4) He created the heavens and the earth
C. The Pen was first; then the Tablet; then the Throne; then water
D. Allah created angels first, then jinn, then the heavens, then Adam
questions.explanation: Imam Al-Bukhari narrated from Imran ibn Husayn: The Prophet ﷺ said to the people of Yemen: '(1) Allah existed and there was nothing else besides Him. (2) His Throne was over the water. (3) He wrote everything in the remembrance (Preserved Tablet). (4) He created the heavens and the earth.' These four statements constitute the foundational framework of creation.

questions.topic: First Creation — Throne, Water, or Pen?
questions.q: There is scholarly debate on whether the Throne, Water, or Pen was created first. What does Dr. Sallabi conclude based on Ibn Hajar and Ibn Kathir?
opts: A. The Pen was definitively the first creation based on a clear hadith with no interpretation needed
B. Water and the Throne are the first of creation; the Pen came after — and the primacy of the Pen in some hadiths refers to everything EXCEPT the Throne and water
C. All three were created simultaneously at the same moment
D. The intellect was the first creation based on a fabricated hadith that scholars rejected
questions.explanation: Ibn Hajar said: The hadith about the Pen being first 'can be reconciled by understanding that the primacy of the Pen refers to everything except water and the Throne.' Ibn Kathir reported that the majority hold the Throne was created before the Pen, based on the hadith: 'Allah recorded the fates of all creatures 50,000 years before creating the heavens and earth, and His Throne was upon water.' Note: The hadith 'The first thing Allah created was the intellect' has NO established chain.

questions.topic: The Throne
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi mentions that Allah created FOUR things with His own hand. What are these four things?
opts: A. The Throne, the Pen, the earth, and the angels
B. The Throne, the Pen, Adam, and the Garden of Eden (Jannatul Khuld)
C. The Throne, water, the soul, and Prophet Muhammad ﷺ
D. The Pen, the Preserved Tablet, Adam, and Hawwa
questions.explanation: Abdullah ibn Umar reported: 'Allah created four things with His hand: the Throne, the Pen, Adam, and the Garden of Eden. Then He said to the rest of creation: Be, and it was.' This was narrated by Al-Darimi, Abu Shaykh, and Al-Lalaka'i with a chain meeting the criteria of Sahih Muslim.

questions.topic: The Throne
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi cites a hadith narrated by Abu Dawood about the Angel carrying the Throne. What astonishing fact is mentioned?
opts: A. The angel's wingspan covers the distance between east and west
B. The distance between the earlobe and shoulder of one of the Throne-bearing angels is a journey of 700 years
C. The angel's voice when glorifying Allah can be heard across all seven heavens
D. The Throne-bearing angels never sleep and have 600 wings
questions.explanation: Abu Dawood narrated: 'I have been permitted to tell about one of Allah's angels who bears the Throne that the distance between the lobe of his ear and his shoulder is a journey of 700 years.' Authenticated by Al-Albani. Dr. Sallabi reflects: 'If this is the description of the DISTANCE BETWEEN EARLOBE AND SHOULDER of ONE angel, what can be said about the Throne itself?'

questions.topic: The Throne – Spiritual Significance
questions.q: On the Day of Judgment when there is no shade, who are the 7 categories Allah will shade under His Throne? (As cited by Dr. Sallabi from Sahih Bukhari/Muslim)
opts: A. The prophets, the martyrs, the scholars, the parents, the orphans, the poor, and the believers

B. A just ruler; a youth who grew up worshiping Allah; one attached to the masjid; two who love each other for Allah; a man who refuses beauty for fear of Allah; one who gives charity secretly; one who remembers Allah alone and weeps
C. Those who fasted Ramadan; performed Hajj; gave Zakat; prayed tahajjud; read Qur'an daily; avoided backbiting; protected their families
D. The sincere, the patient, the grateful, the humble, the truthful, the generous, and the repentant
questions.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Seven people Allah will give them His Shade on the Day when there would be no shade but His Shade: (1) A just ruler; (2) A youth who grew up in worship of Allah; (3) A man whose heart is attached to mosques; (4) Two men who love and depart for Allah's sake; (5) A man who refuses a beautiful woman saying: I fear Allah; (6) One who gives charity so secretly the left hand doesn't know what the right gives; (7) One who remembers Allah in solitude with tearful eyes.'

questions.topic: Darwin's Theory
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi discusses Darwin's theory of evolution extensively. What does he say about its historical misuse?
opts: A. Darwin's theory was used by Islamic scholars to explain the diversity of races

B. Colonialists exploited it claiming the white race was strongest and fittest, leading to destructive ideologies like Nazism and Fascism — even though Darwin himself did not deny the existence of Allah
C. Darwin himself was a Muslim convert at the end of his life
D. The theory was only accepted by Christian missionaries but rejected universally by scientists
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states: 'Darwin claimed to believe in a Creator who gave life, but atheists found in his theory what they were searching for.' Furthermore: 'Colonialists exploited this theory, claiming that the white race was the strongest and fittest, leading to the development of destructive ideologies such as Nazism and Fascism.' The introduction by Al-Qaradaghi elaborates this point extensively.

questions.topic: Darwin's Theory – Scientific Refutation
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi cites scientific evidence against Darwin's theory. What did scientist Charles Eugene calculate about forming a protein molecule by chance?
opts: A. It would require 1 million years — making it highly unlikely
B. The probability of forming a single protein molecule by chance would require a time span of no less than 10,243 million years — far exceeding the Earth's age
C. The odds are 1 in 7 billion, similar to winning a lottery
D. It would require a volume of amino acids that is 500 times the size of the Earth
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi cites: 'Scientist Charles Eugene calculated the probability of forming a single protein molecule by chance and found that it could occur only once in a time span of no less than 10,243 million years — far exceeding the billions of years the Earth has existed.' In 1962, chemists Dixon and Webb found it would require a volume of amino acids 50,000 times the size of the ENTIRE UNIVERSE.

questions.topic: Allah is the First
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi explains the Divine Name 'Al-Awwal' (The First). According to Ibn al-Qayyim he cites, what does worshiping Allah by His name 'The First' require of a servant?
opts: A. Acknowledging that Allah was before all creation in time
B. Detachment from relying on causes and means — recognizing that His grace and mercy alone initiated goodness, with no means from the servant in the state of non-existence before creation
C. Putting Allah before everything in your daily schedule and priorities
D. Making the first action every day a remembrance of Allah
questions.explanation: Ibn al-Qayyim says: 'Worshiping Him by His name The First requires detachment from focusing on the causes and relying on them, and instead recognizing His grace and mercy alone. He is the initiator of goodness without any means from the servant, as there was no means from the servant in the state of non-existence before his creation. What means could there be then? It was nothing but pure non-existence.'

questions.topic: Satanic Whispers about Creation
questions.q: The Prophet ﷺ warned about Satanic whispers regarding creation — specifically about a dangerous question Satan plants in the mind. What is this question, and what is the prescribed remedy?
opts: A. Question: 'Why did Allah create suffering?' — Remedy: Say Alhamdulillah and accept qadr
B. Question: 'Who created Allah?' — Remedy: Seek refuge in Allah, stop thinking about it, say 'I believe in Allah and His messengers,' recite Surah Al-Ikhlas, and spit to the left
C. Question: 'Does Allah really exist?' — Remedy: Perform extra prayers and increase fasting
D. Question: 'Is the Qur'an really from Allah?' — Remedy: Recite Ayat al-Kursi and seek forgiveness
questions.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'People will not stop asking questions till they say: This is Allah, Creator of everything — then who created Allah?' The remedy: 'When he gets to that length the man should seek refuge in Allah and stop thinking about it.' Muslim adds: 'Whoever experiences this should say: I believe in Allah.' Abu Dawood/An-Nasa'i add: 'Recite Surah Al-Ikhlas, then spit to his left, and seek refuge with Allah.'

questions.topic: Duality of Creation
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi discusses the 'Duality of Creation' as evidence of the Oneness of the Creator. Which Qur'anic verse does he cite for this?
opts: A. {And We created the heavens and earth in six days} [Hud: 7]
B. {And of all things We created two mates; perhaps you will remember} [Adh-Dhariyat: 49]
C. {There is nothing like unto Him, and He is the Hearing, the Seeing} [Ash-Shuraa: 11]
D. {Say, He is Allah, the One, the Eternal Refuge} [Al-Ikhlas: 1-2]
questions.explanation: Allah says: {And of all things We created two mates; perhaps you will remember} [Adh-Dhariyat: 49]. Al-Qurtubi explains: 'So that you may know that the Creator of pairs is One, for He is not subject to movement or stillness, light or darkness, sitting or standing, beginning or end.' The duality of creation — sky/earth, night/day, sun/moon, faith/disbelief — points to the Oneness of the Creator.

questions.topic: Wisdom in Creation
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi cites Ibn al-Qayyim's profound discussion on wisdom in creation. What dangerous impact does DENYING wisdom have on religion, according to Ibn al-Qayyim?
opts: A. It leads to increased fatalism and passivity in society
B. Rational people cannot dismiss causes and wisdom from their minds — and when they cannot reconcile the denial of wisdom with divine laws, they cast aside the divine laws altogether
C. It causes people to doubt the existence of Allah entirely
D. It leads to excessive worship based on fear rather than love
questions.explanation: Ibn al-Qayyim said: 'The harm of this statement [denying wisdom] to the divine laws is among the greatest harms. For rational people cannot deny causes, wisdom, benefits, and ultimate purposes. When they see it is impossible to reconcile this with the divine laws and cannot dismiss it from their minds, they cast aside the divine laws, having a negative opinion of them, saying: We cannot reconcile them with our intellects.'

questions.topic: Creation in Stages
questions.q: According to Dr. Sallabi, Allah created the heavens and earth in SIX days. How many days did He use to create the EARTH specifically, according to [Fussilat: 9-10]?
opts: A. One day for the earth itself, and three more for its mountains and sustenance
B. Two days for the earth, then four days for its mountains, sustenance, and blessings — totaling six including the heavens
C. Three days for the earth and three days for everything in it
D. One day for the earth and five days for all living things within it
questions.explanation: Allah says: {Say, Do you indeed disbelieve in He who created the earth in TWO days...? (9) And He placed firmly set mountains over its surface, and blessed it and determined its sustenance in FOUR days...} [Fussilat: 9-10]. Then He directed to the heavens. Total: 2 (earth) + 4 (its provisions/mountains) + creation of heavens = 6 days for the entire creation.

questions.topic: Allah's Self-Sufficiency
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi emphasizes that Allah did NOT create creation out of need. What are the THREE things Allah did NOT need creation for?
opts: A. He did not need worship, praise, or gratitude from creation
B. He did not create creation to seek companionship from loneliness, to increase His numbers from scarcity, or to strengthen Himself from weakness
C. He did not need helpers, partners, or advisors in creation
D. He did not need the heavens, earth, or angels to manage His kingdom
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states: 'One of the key aspects of this grand narrative is that Allah Almighty did not create the creation to seek companionship from loneliness, to increase His numbers from scarcity, or to strengthen Himself from weakness. He is self-sufficient and independent of His creation, while they are in constant need of Him in all their states. He is The Mighty, The Praiseworthy.'

questions.topic: Stages of Adam's Creation
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi lists 6 stages of the creation of Adam ﷺ in Surah Al-A'raf. What are these six stages IN ORDER?
opts: A. Light → Water → Clay → Mud → Form → Soul
B. Dust → Water → Clay → Moulded clay → Dark black mud → Pottery-like clay — then the soul was breathed into this pottery form
C. Sand → Wet clay → Sticky clay → Fired clay → Living form → Soul
D. Earth → Sounding clay → Altered mud → Black clay → Smooth clay → Living being
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi explains from Surah Al-A'raf: Stage 1: DUST; Stage 2: WATER; Stage 3: CLAY; Stage 4: MOULDED CLAY; Stage 5: DARK, BLACK MUD; Stage 6: POTTERY-like clay. After these stages, Adam ﷺ became a lifeless, molded form. This was the formation phase in preparation for the breathing of the soul by Allah: {when I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My [created] soul} [Sad: 71-72].
questions.source: Chapter Two, Section Three (Adam in Al-A'raf) – pp.19-20

questions.topic: The Soul
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi makes a profound statement about the honor of a human being in relation to the soul. What is it?
opts: A. 'The honor of a human being is in their lineage and their deeds'

B. 'The honor of a human being is in the breath of the soul, not in the handful of clay. Religion is the soul of the soul, so the honor of the human being is in both the soul and the soul of the soul.'
C. 'The honor of a human being comes from being made from the earth and returning to it'
D. 'The honor of a human is in their mind, which distinguishes them from animals'
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi writes: 'The honor of a human being is embodied in the breath of the soul, not in the handful of clay. The clay's value is elevated by the breath of the soul, the recognition of the Noble Creator, and the great religion. Religion is the soul of the soul, so the honor of the human being is found in both the soul and the soul of the soul. Reflect on this!'

questions.topic: Teaching Adam the Names
questions.q: What does Dr. Sallabi say about the significance of Allah teaching Adam ﷺ 'all the names' [Al-Baqarah: 31]?
opts: A. It means Allah taught Adam all human languages simultaneously
B. It demonstrates Adam's superiority over the angels — the intellect as the primary tool for learning, freedom of expression, and the origin of language are all connected to this miracle
C. It means Adam was given the gift of prophecy before any other prophet
D. It showed that knowledge of creation is a form of worship of the Creator
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi notes that the teaching of names to Adam ﷺ reveals: the intellect as the primary tool for learning, the origin of language, insights into unseen miracles, the reason Adam was given that name, and how freedom of expression manifested in him. When Adam named the things before the angels, it demonstrated his superiority over them — he knew what they did not.

questions.topic: Iblees
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi addresses whether Iblees was from the JINN or the ANGELS. What does he conclude based on the Qur'anic verses?
opts: A. Iblees was definitively an angel who became corrupted
B. The Qur'anic evidence indicates Iblees was from the jinn — specifically cited from Surah Al-Kahf: {he was of the jinn and departed from the command of his Lord}
C. Iblees was a hybrid creation — neither fully angel nor fully jinn
D. The matter is uncertain and scholars have no clear position
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi discusses this in the sections on Adam in Surah Al-Baqarah and Al-Kahf: Allah says {except Iblees. He was of the jinn and departed from the command of his Lord} [Al-Kahf: 50]. This establishes Iblees as being from the jinn — created from smokeless fire — not from the angels who are created from light. His being commanded with the angels was due to his worship and elevated station among them, not his nature.

questions.topic: Iblees – Arrogance
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi discusses arrogance as the root sin of Iblees. What was the ESSENCE of Iblees's argument when he refused to prostrate to Adam ﷺ?
opts: A. He claimed Adam was not worthy because he was made of clay and was new
B. He compared himself to Adam and believed he was more honourable — 'I am better than him; You created me from fire and created him from clay' — making Qiyas (analogy) against a divine command
C. He argued that prostrating to anyone other than Allah is shirk
D. He claimed the other angels should not prostrate either since Adam was not a prophet yet
questions.explanation: Ibn Sirin said: 'Iblees tried to compare himself to Adam. He believed he was more honourable than Adam. Therefore he abstained from prostrating even though Allah had commanded him.' Iblees said: 'I am not the one to prostrate to a human being whom You created from sounding clay of altered black smooth mud.' He made a false analogy — fire is superior to clay — but missed the spiritual honor Allah gave Adam: the divine soul, knowledge of names, and divine selection.
questions.source: Chapter Two (Adam in Al-A'raf) – based on Qur'an [Al-A'raf: 11-13]

questions.topic: Iblees – Methods of Misguidance
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi lists 6 specific methods Iblees uses to misguide humanity, from Surah Al-Hijr. What are they?
opts: A. Lying, stealing, murder, fornication, magic, and polytheism

B. (1) Doubting the Creator's existence; (2) Questioning Allah's right to exclusive worship/encouraging idols; (3) Doubting faith-based doctrines; (4) General skepticism about Muslim life; (5) Beautifying desires and sins; (6) Discouraging righteous deeds
C. Pride, envy, greed, anger, lust, laziness
D. Whispers in prayer, bad dreams, division among believers, financial corruption, doubts about the Qur'an, doubts about the Prophet ﷺ
questions.explanation: From Surah Al-Hijr: Iblees said {I will surely make disobedience attractive to them on earth, and I will mislead them all} [Al-Hijr: 39]. Dr. Sallabi details his 6 methods: (1) Doubt about Creator; (2) Question exclusive worship/promote idols; (3) Doubt doctrines; (4) General skepticism about Muslim life; (5) Beautify sins/desires; (6) Discourage righteous deeds. The antidote: Sincerity — 'except Your sincere servants' [Al-Hijr: 40].

questions.topic: Adam's Repentance
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi discusses the KEY difference between Adam ﷺ's disobedience and Iblees's disobedience. What is the fundamental difference?
opts: A. Adam's sin was greater but forgiven due to his prophetic status
B. Adam ﷺ sinned, recognized his error, repented immediately, and was guided by Allah to words of repentance — while Iblees sinned through arrogance, refused to repent, and persisted in his rebellion
C. Adam's disobedience was accidental due to forgetfulness while Iblees acted deliberately
D. The difference is only a matter of degree — both committed the same type of sin
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi highlights: 'I emphasized the wisdom behind Adam's disobedience and his subsequent repentance, highlighting the difference between Adam's disobedience and that of Iblees.' Adam: {Then Adam received from his Lord some words, and He accepted his repentance} [Al-Baqarah: 37]. Iblees: arrogance, no repentance, eternal enmity. This also shows there is NO concept of inherited sin in Islam.

questions.topic: No Original Sin in Islam
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi explicitly states an important contrast with Christian theology regarding Adam's sin. What does he clarify?
opts: A. Islam agrees with Christianity that all humans carry the guilt of Adam's sin
B. 'There is no concept of inherited sin in Islam' — Adam repented, was forgiven, and his descendants do not carry guilt for his sin
C. Islam teaches that Adam's sin only affects those who repeat it
D. The concept of original sin applies only to the Children of Israel
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states clearly: 'I also clarified that there is no concept of inherited sin in Islam.' Adam ﷺ repented, Allah accepted his repentance: {Indeed, it is He who is the Accepting of Repentance, the Merciful} [Al-Baqarah: 37]. Each person is accountable only for their own deeds: {And no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another} [Al-An'am: 164].

questions.topic: The Garden of Adam
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi notes that the 'garden' where Adam ﷺ lived before the descent is discussed. What does he address regarding the tree Adam was instructed to avoid?
opts: A. The tree was a fig tree and represents the cycle of seasons
B. He addresses which garden it was, the specific tree they were instructed to avoid 'in order to prevent wrongdoing,' and that they were warned about their enemy Iblees
C. The tree was purely symbolic and represents forbidden knowledge
D. The tree's fruit gave eternal life and was therefore kept exclusively for Allah
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states: 'I addressed the garden mentioned in the story, where Adam and Eve were permitted to live, as well as the specific tree they were instructed to avoid in order to prevent wrongdoing. They were also warned about their enemy, Iblees.' The entry points of Satan through instincts — specifically the instincts of KINGSHIP and IMMORTALITY — were how he deceived them.
questions.source: Chapter Two (Adam in Al-Baqarah and Ta-Ha) – pp.17-18, 25

questions.topic: Adam's Descent to Earth
questions.q: According to Dr. Sallabi, the descent of Adam, Eve, and Iblees to earth was not a punishment without purpose. What wisdom does he cite for the descent?
opts: A. The earth needed human custodians to build civilization and return to Allah
B. Adam and his wife were prepared to cultivate the earth — it is the 'grand home' — and the earth needed its vicegerent (khalifah) as originally announced to the angels
C. The descent was to test humanity before granting them Paradise permanently
D. Both A and B — the descent fulfilled the original divine plan of creating a khalifah on earth
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states: 'I discussed the descent of Adam and his wife, as well as Iblees, to earth, and the wisdom behind this descent. I also explained that Adam and his wife were prepared to cultivate the earth, as it is our grand home.' This fulfills the original divine announcement to the angels: {I am going to place a vicegerent on earth} [Al-Baqarah: 30].

questions.topic: Adam's Prophethood
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi addresses whether Adam ﷺ was a PROPHET and MESSENGER. What evidence does he provide for Adam's prophethood?
opts: A. Adam is not mentioned among the prophets in the Qur'an so his prophethood is disputed

B. Multiple evidences: (1) Allah called him multiple times and spoke to him directly in Paradise; (2) Taught him all names; (3) He repented inspired by Allah; (4) He met the Prophet ﷺ in the lowest heaven during Isra — only prophets were encountered there; (5) All sects of Muslims unanimously count him among prophets
C. Adam was only a prophet after his descent to earth, not while in Paradise
D. His prophethood is proven only by the hadith of intercession on the Day of Judgment
questions.explanation: From Sallabi's article on the site: Allah called Adam multiple times in Paradise — this alone is sufficient for prophethood. The Prophet ﷺ met Adam in the lowest heaven during Mi'raj and only prophets were encountered. Additionally: {Indeed, Allah chose Adam and Noah and the family of Abraham and the family of Imran over the worlds} [Ali 'Imran: 33]. All sects of Muslims agree he is among the prophets and messengers.
questions.source: Chapter Two (Adam's Prophethood) – based on book content pp.689-672

questions.topic: Eve (Hawwa)
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi discusses the creation of Eve (Hawwa) and her role. What does he say her creation was for?
opts: A. Eve was created to be a temptress to test Adam's obedience
B. Allah created Eve as a companion to Adam to share in life and help establish the FIRST BUILDING BLOCK of human civilization and its expansion
C. Eve was created only after Adam had lived alone for many years in the garden
D. Eve was created to show the equality of men and women before Allah
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states: 'I discussed how Allah created our mother, Eve, as a companion to our father, Adam, to share in life and help establish the first building block of human civilization and its expansion.' The book also addresses marriage: 'The Holy Qur'an established the institution of marriage for Adam to be a divine tradition for humanity, aimed at providing companionship, love, fulfilling desires, and bearing offspring.'

questions.topic: Marriage as Divine Institution
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi emphasizes marriage as a divine institution established from Adam ﷺ. What Qur'anic verse does he cite to show the universal nature of this institution?
opts: A. {And We created you in pairs} [An-Naba: 8]
B. {And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquility in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy} [Ar-Rum: 21]
C. {So direct your face toward the religion, inclining to truth. The fitrah of Allah upon which He has created all people} [Ar-Rum: 30]
D. {And marry the unmarried among you} [An-Nur: 32]
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states from the book: 'The Holy Qur'an established the institution of marriage for Adam ﷺ to be a divine tradition for humanity... It serves as the exclusive, pure, and blessed lawful means for the union of male and female.' He cites {And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquility in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy} [Ar-Rum: 21].

questions.topic: Human Civilization
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi outlines the foundations of early human civilization under Adam ﷺ. Which of the following is NOT listed among the topics he covers in the eighth section?
opts: A. Marriage and Procreation; Provision of Life's Needs; Adam as Caller of Monotheism
B. Adam and the Sacred House (Ka'bah); Moral Foundation; The Stewardship of Earth
C. The Story of Cain and Abel; Human Dignity; Concept of Trust (Amanah)
D. The story of Noah's Ark and the first global flood under Adam's time
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi's eighth section on Adam's descent covers: Beginning of Human Civilization, Marriage and Procreation, Provision of Life's Needs, Adam as Caller of Monotheism, Worship of Allah on Earth, Adam and the Sacred House, Moral Foundation, Principles of Early Human Ethics, Stewardship of Earth, Divine Methodology, Succession, Trust (Amanah), Human Dignity, Physical Honor, Intellectual Honor, The Story of Cain and Abel, and The Death of Adam. Noah's flood is a separate era.

questions.topic: Surah Al-Isra and Adam
questions.q: In his section on Adam in Surah Al-Isra, Dr. Sallabi focuses on a concluding verse. What verse does he deeply reflect on?
opts: A. {We have certainly honored the children of Adam} [Al-Isra: 70]
B. {And sufficient is your Lord as Disposer of affairs} [Al-Isra: 65] — and the name 'Al-Wakeel' (Disposer of Affairs) and its connection to the story
C. {And We said to the angels, Prostrate to Adam, and they prostrated} [Al-Isra: 61]
D. {And He taught Adam the names, all of them} [Al-Isra: 33]
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states: 'I focused on the concluding verse of the story in Surah Al-Isra: {And sufficient is your Lord as Disposer of affairs} [Al-Isra: 65]. I reflected deeply on the name of Allah — The Disposer of Affairs — and its connection to the events of the story, considering its impact on human life in both this world and the Hereafter.'

questions.topic: Surah Sad and Creation
questions.q: In the section on Adam in Surah Sad, Dr. Sallabi discusses a unique concept. What is the 'celestial assembly' (Al-Mala' Al-A'la) dialogue he refers to?
opts: A. The dialogue between the angels and Iblees after the prostration
B. The dialogue that occurred in the highest assembly related to the creation of Adam — which can only be accessed through the Holy Qur'an revealed to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ
C. The conversation between Adam and Iblees in Paradise before the fall
D. The celestial meeting where the Preserved Tablet was revealed to the angels
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states about Surah Sad: 'I discussed the beginning of humanity and the dialogue that occurred in the celestial assembly related to the creation of Adam (peace be upon him), which can only be accessed through the Holy Qur'an revealed to our truthful and infallible Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him).' The verses in Sad 38:69-76 contain this celestial discussion.
questions.source: Chapter Two (Adam in Surah Sad) – p.25-26

questions.topic: The Name Allah
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi lists several unique qualities of the Name 'Allah'. Which of the following is one of them?
opts: A. It is the name used most frequently by non-Muslims referring to God

B. It is a name that belongs to Him alone — no one else is associated with it, it is not applied to anyone else, and none of His creation claims it; it is also considered by some to be His Greatest Name
C. It has 7 letters in Arabic representing the 7 heavens
D. It is only used in formal prayer and not in everyday speech
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi lists: 'Allah' is a name that (1) belongs to Him alone — no creature is named by it; (2) is considered by some to be His Greatest Name; (3) is the most frequently mentioned name in Qur'an and Sunnah; (4) is the most famous name spoken by all creatures in various languages; (5) signifies the magnificent essence encompassing all divine attributes; (6) is associated with all worlds in their essence; (7) encompasses all divine names.

questions.topic: Loyalty and Disavowal (Al-Wala' wal-Bara')
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi cites Imam Ahmad's warning about 'loyalty and disavowal' as a SLOGAN. What did Imam Ahmad say?
opts: A. Loyalty to believers and disavowal of disbelievers is obligatory for every Muslim
B. 'Loyalty is an innovation, and disavowal is an innovation' — referring to making it a banner for declaring Muslims as disbelievers and fighting them, as the Kharijites did
C. The concept is from the Sunnah and must be taught to every Muslim child
D. Loyalty and disavowal are the two pillars of the testimony of faith
questions.explanation: Imam Ahmad said (in the narration of al-Istukhri): 'Loyalty is an innovation, and disavowal is an innovation; they are the ones who say: We befriend so-and-so and disassociate from so-and-so — and this statement is an innovation, so beware of it.' This was directed at those who turned it into a slogan to excommunicate Muslims. Dr. Sallabi also cites Al-Qaradaghi's distinction: disavowal is from ACTIONS, not from persons.

questions.topic: Allah is Self-Sufficient
questions.q: How many times does the name 'Al-Ghani' (The Self-Sufficient) appear in the Holy Qur'an, and how is it most often paired, according to Dr. Sallabi?
opts: A. 7 times — most often with Al-Aziz (The Mighty)
B. 18 times — most often with Al-Hamid (The Praiseworthy) as in {Allah is the Free of need, the Praiseworthy}
C. 99 times — as one of the 99 Names of Allah
D. 33 times — paired with Al-Wahhab (The Bestower)
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states: 'Among the beautiful names of Allah is The Self-Sufficient (Al-Ghani), which is mentioned in the Holy Qur'an eighteen times; sometimes alone as in [Yunus: 68], and sometimes combined with His name The Praiseworthy — which is the most common — as in {O mankind, you are those in need of Allah, while Allah is the Free of need, the Praiseworthy} [Fatir: 15].'

questions.topic: The Preserved Tablet and Pen
questions.q: What did Allah command the Pen to write, according to the hadith narrated by Imam Ahmad, Abu Dawood, and Al-Tirmidhi?
opts: A. The 99 Names of Allah and their meanings
B. 'Write what was decreed about everything till the Last Hour comes' — meaning all decrees and destinies until the Day of Judgment
C. The Qur'an before it was revealed to Muhammad ﷺ
D. The names and destinies of all human beings who would ever live
questions.explanation: The hadith of Ubadah ibn al-Samit: 'The first thing Allah created was the pen. He said to it: Write. It asked: What should I write, my Lord? He said: Write what was decreed about everything till the Last Hour comes.' This establishes that all of creation's destiny — past, present, future — was written in the Preserved Tablet.

questions.topic: Creation and Resurrection
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi cites a hadith about a person who denied the resurrection. What does the hadith say Allah responds about the comparison between first creation and resurrection?
opts: A. 'Creation from nothing is easy for Me — restoring it is even easier'
B. 'The son of Adam denied Me saying I will not resurrect him as I created him — but resurrecting him is not more difficult for Me than creating him in the first place'
C. 'If I created the seven heavens and earth in 6 days, I can recreate him in one moment'
D. 'Does he not know that I created him from a drop of fluid and then from a clinging substance?'
questions.explanation: The hadith narrated from Abu Huraira: 'Allah says: The son of Adam denied Me and he had no right to do so... As for his denying Me, it is his saying that I will not resurrect him as I created him in the beginning, but resurrecting him is NOT more difficult for Me than creating him in the first place.' [Al-Anbya: 104]: {As We began the first creation, We will repeat it}.

questions.topic: The Throne – Souls of Martyrs
questions.q: Dr. Sallabi cites a hadith about where the souls of MARTYRS reside. Where are they, according to the hadith from Sahih Muslim?
opts: A. In a meadow in the highest Paradise (Firdaws)
B. Inside green birds having lanterns suspended from the Throne, roaming freely in Paradise where they please, then returning to those lanterns
C. Resting in the shade of the Throne awaiting the Day of Resurrection
D. Near the graves of their bodies, free to visit wherever they wish
questions.explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said in Sahih Muslim: 'Their souls are in the insides of green birds having lanterns suspended from the Throne, roaming freely in Paradise where they please, then taking shelter in those lanterns. Their Lord cast a glance at them, and said: Do you wish for anything? They said: What shall we wish for when we roam freely in Paradise where we please?' Three times they were asked, then they wished to fight for Allah again.

questions.topic: La ilaha illa Allah – Loyalty and Disavowal
questions.q: What is Dr. Sallabi's key point about HOW La ilaha illa Allah united people of different nations?
opts: A. It united them through a shared Arabic language
B. La ilaha illa Allah united Suhayb the Roman, Bilal the Ethiopian, Salman the Persian, and Abu Bakr the Qurashi Arab — tribal, racial, and territorial loyalties faded under the banner of monotheism
C. It united them through a shared political system and caliphate
D. It united them through the Hajj pilgrimage to the Ka'bah every year
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi states: 'The declaration of La ilaha illa Allah united Suhayb the Roman, Bilal the Ethiopian, Salman the Persian, and Abu Bakr the Qurashi Arab. Tribal, racial, and territorial loyalties faded. The Messenger ﷺ said: Leave it, for it is something disgusting — referring to party spirit (asabiyyah). He also said: He who dies upholding party spirit does not belong to us.'

questions.topic: Adam's Death and Legacy
questions.q: What was Adam ﷺ's PRIMARY mission to his descendants after his descent to earth, according to Dr. Sallabi?
opts: A. Teaching them agriculture and how to cultivate the earth
B. Acting as a Caller to Monotheism (Da'i ila al-Tawhid) and Divine Guidance — establishing worship of Allah on earth as his central mission
C. Building the first cities and establishing the first laws of governance
D. Carrying the divine scriptures and distributing them to all his children
questions.explanation: Dr. Sallabi includes 'Adam as a Caller of Monotheism and Divine Guidance' and 'Worship of Allah on Earth' as central topics in the section on Adam's life on earth. He also discusses 'Adam and the Sacred House,' showing his connection to the Ka'bah and the foundational Tawhid-based civilization. The moral foundation of early human civilization was built on the Oneness of Allah.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What is the distance between each heaven and the next, according to the hadith of Abu Dharr al-Ghifari?
questions.explanation: The hadith of Abu Dharr al-Ghifari describes the distance between each of the seven heavens as five hundred years of traveling, emphasizing the vastness and grandeur of the universe created by Allah. This measurement highlights the immense scale beyond human comprehension.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: From what substance were the angels created, according to the authentic hadith cited by Ibn Kathir from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her)?
questions.explanation: Angels were created from light (nur), as mentioned in authentic hadith narrated by Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). This distinguishes them from humans, who were created from clay, and jinn, who were created from smokeless fire.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: How many angels carry the Throne of Allah on the Day of Judgment, as mentioned in the Quran and explained by Ibn Kathir?
questions.explanation: On the Day of Judgment, eight angels will carry the Throne of Allah, as mentioned in the Quran (Surah Al-Haaqqa 69:17) and explained by Ibn Kathir, highlighting their immense strength and the significance of that moment in Islamic eschatology.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What is the name of the angel specifically mentioned as the keeper of the "Bayt al-Ma'mur" in the lowest heaven?
questions.explanation: The angel specifically mentioned as the keeper of the "Bayt al-Ma'mur" is not Ishmael (Isma'il), but rather it is an angel assigned to this heavenly sanctuary where many angels worship. Ishmael is a prophet, not an angel, so the keeper is a distinct angelic being responsible for this sacred place.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: How many angels enter the Bayt al-Ma'mur daily, never to return?
questions.explanation: Seventy thousand angels enter the Bayt al-Ma'mur daily, and each group never returns, as this heavenly house is visited only once by each angel. This is based on Islamic teachings describing the unique and continuous worship occurring in the highest heaven.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of Adam ﷺ
questions.q: In the hadith where the Prophet ﷺ asks his companions about what is above them, what answer does he give when they say "Allah and His Messenger know best"?
opts: "It is Al-Rafi` (the canopy), a protected ceiling and a withheld wave"
"It is the highest heaven where Adam resides"
questions.explanation: When the companions say "Allah and His Messenger know best," the Prophet ﷺ explains that above them is Al-Rafi`—a protected canopy or ceiling that shields and preserves the heavens, emphasizing Allah’s control and protection over the universe’s structure.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: According to the same hadith, what is the distance between the seventh heaven and the Throne?
opts: Five hundred years of traveling, similar to the distance between heavens
questions.explanation: The hadith describes the distance between the seventh heaven and the Throne as five hundred years of traveling, emphasizing the vastness and majesty of Allah’s creation. This measure is similar to the distances between the heavens themselves, illustrating the immense scale of the universe beyond human comprehension.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What is the distance between each earth and the next, according to the hadith of Abu Dharr?
opts: The same distance as between the heavens: five hundred years
questions.explanation: The hadith of Abu Dharr describes the vastness of creation by stating that the distance between each earth and the next is equivalent to five hundred years of traveling, emphasizing the immense scale Allah has created beyond human comprehension.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What is the primary characteristic of the angels in charge of Hellfire, as described in Surah al-Tahrim quoted by Ibn Kathir?
opts: They are harsh and severe, and they do not disobey Allah
They are invisible to the inhabitants of Hell
questions.explanation: Angels assigned to Hellfire are described as harsh and severe because they strictly carry out Allah's commands without question, reflecting their unwavering obedience and the seriousness of their duty in administering punishment. This highlights their role in executing divine justice with absolute submission.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: According to Ibn Kathir's commentary, why did the angels appear in this form to Lut?
opts: Because they could not appear in their true form
As a test for Lut and his people
Because Lut requested to see them that way
To hide from Iblis who was following them
questions.explanation: The angels appeared in human form to Lut as a test to see how he and his people would respond, revealing their true character and obedience to Allah's commands. This trial was meant to distinguish the righteous from the sinful.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What is the function of the "Kiraman Katibin" (Noble Recorders)?
opts: They guard the gates of Paradise
They record every deed of every person
They question the dead in their graves
questions.explanation: The Kiraman Katibin are two angels assigned to each person; one records good deeds and the other records bad deeds. Their role is to document every action, ensuring that nothing is missed, which will be used on the Day of Judgment to assess a person's accountability.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Creation of the Earth
questions.q: According to one narration cited by Ibn Kathir from Shahr ibn Hawshab, what was the original situation of the Jinn on earth, and what happened to them?
opts: They were obedient until Iblis corrupted them
They caused corruption on earth, so Allah sent angels who fought them and drove them to the islands of the seas
They were created after humans and lived peacefully
They were all destroyed except Iblis who repented
questions.explanation: According to Ibn Kathir's narration from Shahr ibn Hawshab, the Jinn initially lived on earth and caused corruption, prompting Allah to send angels who fought them and expelled them to the islands in the seas, illustrating divine intervention to maintain order on earth.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: What did Allah teach Adam that He did not teach the angels, thereby demonstrating Adam's superiority?
opts: The stories of the past prophets
questions.explanation: Allah taught Adam the names of all things, which the angels did not know, demonstrating Adam's unique knowledge and superiority. This knowledge symbolizes human capacity for learning and understanding beyond the angels' abilities.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · Angels
questions.q: How many angels carry the Throne on the Day of Judgment, according to Surah al-Haqqah?
questions.explanation: Surah al-Haqqah (69:17) mentions that eight angels will carry the Throne of Allah on the Day of Judgment, highlighting their immense strength and the significance of that day. This number symbolizes the majesty and power involved in the divine event.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What did Ibn Abbas say about the Kursi in relation to the heavens?
opts: The Kursi is the seventh heaven
The Kursi is the place of the feet, and the Throne is above it, and the heavens compared to the Kursi are like a ring in a desert
The Kursi is smaller than the first heaven
The Kursi is the Throne itself
questions.explanation: Ibn Abbas explained that the Kursi represents the place of Allah’s feet, positioned beneath the Throne, which is above it. He illustrated the vastness of the heavens by comparing them to a small ring in a desert when set against the immense size of the Kursi.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: What does the Verse of the Kursi (Ayat al-Kursi) describe about Allah's sovereignty?
opts: That His Throne extends over the heavens and the earth
That He sits on the Throne
That the Throne is limited to the seventh heaven
That the Kursi is His dwelling place
questions.explanation: Ayat al-Kursi describes Allah's sovereignty by stating that His Throne extends over the heavens and the earth, symbolizing His supreme authority, control, and dominion over all creation. This illustrates His unmatched power and knowledge encompassing everything in existence.

questions.topic: Beginning of Time · The Throne & The Kursi
questions.q: How many heavens did Allah create?
